dimensions.label: Completeness
dimensions.description: Covers requirements, ASRs, and quality scenarios.

dimensions.label: Faithfulness
dimensions.description: Avoids hallucinated components, relations, technologies, and unsupported assumptions.

dimensions.label: Architectural Rationality
dimensions.description: Uses reasonable responsibilities, dependencies, boundaries, and quality-attribute handling.

dimensions.label: Traceability
dimensions.description: Links key elements and decisions back to requirements or project evidence.

dimensions.label: Readability
dimensions.description: Presents a clear, reviewable architecture view.

samples.title: s000038_2c7731fc / direct/gpt-5
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false
left to right direction
title IceFireDB Decentralized Database Infrastructure - System Architecture

' External clients
node "Redis-based Application\n(Client uses Redis protocol)" as RedisApp
node "Pub/Sub App (Publisher)" as PubApp
node "Pub/Sub App (Subscriber)" as SubApp

' IceFireDB Redis Proxy subsystem
package "IceFireDB - Redis Proxy" as RedisProxyPkg {
  component "Redis Proxy Agent A" as RPA
  component "Redis Proxy Agent B" as RPB
}

cloud "Redis Agent Sync Network\n(Instruction synchronization across agents)" as AgentNet

database "Redis Storage\n(Standalone or Cluster)" as RedisDB

' IceFireDB PubSub subsystem
package "IceFireDB - PubSub" as PubSubPkg {
  frame "Site A (Private/NAT)" as SiteA {
    component "PubSub Node A" as PSA
  }
  frame "Site B (Private/NAT)" as SiteB {
    component "PubSub Node B" as PSB
  }
}

cloud "P2P Pub/Sub Network\nKademlia DHT + libp2p/IPFS-style\nPeer discovery & routing" as PubSubNet

' Internal SQLite-related path (non top-level feature)
package "IceFireDB - SQLite (internal)" as SQLitePkg {
  component "SQL/MySQL Result Parser\n- Fields & metadata\n- Rows\n- Warnings & status" as SQLRes
}

' Connectivity: Redis-based application to Redis Proxy
RedisApp --> RPA : Redis protocol\n(commands/instructions)

' Redis Proxy Agents: synchronization and storage forwarding
RPA <..> AgentNet : synchronize\nsupported instructions
RPB <..> AgentNet : synchronize\nsupported instructions

RPA --> RedisDB : Forward reads/writes\ncluster-aware when configured
RPB --> RedisDB : Forward reads/writes\ncluster-aware when configured

' Pub/Sub: clients and nodes
PubApp --> PSA : PUBLISH\n(Redis-style semantics)
SubApp --> PSB : SUBSCRIBE\n(Redis-style semantics)

PSA <..> PubSubNet : Pub/Sub messages\nPeer discovery & routing
PSB <..> PubSubNet : Pub/Sub messages\nPeer discovery & routing

' Notes capturing key requirements and constraints
note right of RPA
- Proxies Redis-backed application traffic (FR-001)
- Synchronizes supported commands across agents (FR-002, FR-004)
- Forwards writes to configured Redis storage (FR-003)
- Cluster-aware routing for Redis Cluster (FR-010)
- Supports command categories:
  String, Set, List, Hash, Sorted Set, Stream, basic control (FR-011)
Compatibility: Standalone & Clustered Redis (NFR-001)
end note

note right of RedisDB
Represents configured Redis target:
- Standalone OR Cluster (FR-001, NFR-001)
- Cluster-state/failover behavior to be validated (NFR-003)
end note

note bottom of PubSubNet
Decentralized Pub/Sub:
- Redis publish/subscribe semantics (FR-005, FR-006, NFR-002)
- Multi-node across same/cross/NAT/private networks (FR-007, FR-008, NFR-004)
- Kademlia DHT with libp2p/IPFS-style discovery & routing (FR-009, C-003)
end note

note bottom of SQLitePkg
Internal data handling only (C-006):
Parses packetized SQL/MySQL-style result data:
- Fields/metadata, rows, warnings, status
(Not a top-level product feature within this scope)
end note
@enduml
samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #333333
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "Web2 application stack" as Web2Stack {
    rectangle "Applications" as Web2Applications
    rectangle "Platform" as Web2Platform
    rectangle "Processing\nBusiness Logic" as Web2Processing
    rectangle "File System\nLarge Files and Media" as Web2FileSystem
    database "BlockChain Database\nTransactions, Contracts,\nCertificates and Receipts" as Web2BlockchainDatabase
    rectangle "IceFireDB" as Web2IceFireDB
}

package "Web3 application stack" as Web3Stack {
    rectangle "Applications" as Web3Applications
    rectangle "Platform" as Web3Platform
    rectangle "Decentralized Processing\nBusiness Logic" as Web3Processing
    rectangle "Decentralized File System\nLarge Files and Media" as Web3FileSystem
    database "BlockChain Database\nTransactions, Contracts,\nCertificates and Receipts" as Web3BlockchainDatabase
    rectangle "IceFireDB" as Web3IceFireDB
}

Web2Applications --> Web2Platform
Web2Platform --> Web2Processing
Web2Processing --> Web2FileSystem
Web2Processing --> Web2BlockchainDatabase
Web2BlockchainDatabase --> Web2IceFireDB

Web3Applications --> Web3Platform
Web3Platform --> Web3Processing
Web3Processing --> Web3FileSystem
Web3Processing --> Web3BlockchainDatabase
Web3BlockchainDatabase --> Web3IceFireDB

Web2IceFireDB ..> Web3IceFireDB : decentralized database support
@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The diagram comprehensively captures all functional areas defined in the SRS, including the Redis Proxy subsystem, the decentralized Pub/Sub subsystem, and the internal SQLite-related MySQL protocol parser. Key architectural constraints and environments (NAT, clustered Redis, agent synchronization) are well represented.

faithfulness.reasoning: The architecture strictly adheres to the provided SRS. It correctly restricts the SQLite functionality to internal data handling as mandated by C-006, and correctly implements the P2P networking concepts (Kademlia DHT, libp2p/IPFS-style) without introducing unsupported IPFS file-storage concepts, abiding by C-003.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The architectural layout is highly logical. It effectively separates the proxying/synchronization plane (Redis Agents + Sync Network) from the storage plane (Redis DB). The Pub/Sub network is adequately modeled with NAT/private network boundaries, demonstrating an understanding of decentralized topologies.

traceability.reasoning: Traceability is exceptional. The diagram includes explicit annotations mapping components, connections, and constraints directly back to the SRS requirement IDs (e.g., FR-001 to FR-011, NFR-001 to NFR-004, and Constraints).

readability.reasoning: The diagram is well-structured, utilizing distinct packages, nodes, and cloud shapes to differentiate between applications, proxies, networks, and storage. The use of notes is clean and provides necessary context without cluttering the structural lines.

samples.title: s000054_5d13634b / direct/claude-sonnet-4-6
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
!define RECTANGLE class

skinparam backgroundColor #FAFAFA
skinparam component {
  BackgroundColor #DDEEFF
  BorderColor #336699
  FontColor #000000
}
skinparam node {
  BackgroundColor #FFF8DC
  BorderColor #B8860B
}
skinparam database {
  BackgroundColor #E8F5E9
  BorderColor #388E3C
}
skinparam rectangle {
  BackgroundColor #F3E5F5
  BorderColor #7B1FA2
}
skinparam cloud {
  BackgroundColor #E3F2FD
  BorderColor #1565C0
}
skinparam arrow {
  Color #333333
}
skinparam defaultFontSize 12

title tokencard/contracts — System Architecture

' ─── External Actors ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
actor "Owner\n(Externally Owned Address)" as Owner
actor "Controller Addresses\n(Service / Confirmation)" as Controller
actor "Integrator / Developer\n(Go Binding Consumer)" as Integrator

' ─── Ethereum Network Layer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cloud "Ethereum Network" as Ethereum {

  ' ─── ENS Subsystem ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  node "Ethereum Name Service (ENS)" as ENS {
    component "ENS Resolver" as ENSResolver
  }

  ' ─── Core Smart Contracts ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  node "Consumer Contract Wallet" as WalletNode {
    component "Wallet Core" as WalletCore
    database "ETH Holdings" as ETHHoldings
    database "ERC20 Token Holdings" as ERC20Holdings
    WalletCore --> ETHHoldings
    WalletCore --> ERC20Holdings
  }

  node "Controller Contract" as ControllerNode {
    component "Controller Logic" as ControllerLogic
    component "adminCount : uint256" as AdminCount
    component "controllerCount : uint256" as ControllerCount
    ControllerLogic --> AdminCount
    ControllerLogic --> ControllerCount
  }

  node "Token Whitelist Contract" as WhitelistNode {
    component "Whitelist Registry" as WhitelistRegistry
    database "Token Entry\n(exchange rate,\nloadable, burnable)" as TokenEntry
    WhitelistRegistry --> TokenEntry
  }

  node "Oracle / Rate Provider Contract" as OracleNode {
    component "Exchange Rate Provider" as RateProvider
    database "Exchange Rate Data" as RateData
    RateProvider --> RateData
  }

  node "Licence Contract" as LicenceNode {
    component "Licence Logic" as LicenceLogic
  }

  node "TKN Holder Contract" as TKNHolder {
    component "Burn Eligibility Logic" as BurnLogic
  }

}

' ─── Gas Tank (Off-Wallet) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
node "Gas Tank\n(on Owner Address,\noutside Wallet Contract)" as GasTank

' ─── Go Binding Layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
node "Generated Go Bindings" as GoBIndingsNode {
  component "Controller Binding" as ControllerBinding
  component "Wallet Binding" as WalletBinding
  component "Generic Binding\n(Call / Transact / Transfer)" as GenericBinding
}

' ─── ENS Resolution Flows ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
WalletCore ..> ENSResolver : resolve controller address\n(FR-002)
WalletCore ..> ENSResolver : resolve token whitelist address\n(FR-002)
WalletCore ..> ENSResolver : resolve oracle/licence address\n(FR-002)
ENSResolver ..> ControllerNode : returns resolved address
ENSResolver ..> WhitelistNode : returns resolved address
ENSResolver ..> OracleNode : returns resolved address
ENSResolver ..> LicenceNode : returns resolved address

' ─── Wallet Internal Flows ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
WalletCore --> WhitelistRegistry : query token loadability,\nexchange rate, burnability\n(FR-003)
WalletCore --> RateProvider : obtain exchange-rate data\n(FR-001)
WalletCore --> ControllerLogic : controller / confirmation\noperations

' ─── TKN Burn Flow ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
WhitelistRegistry --> BurnLogic : burnability status\n(FR-003)
TKNHolder --> WhitelistNode : check burnable flag

' ─── Gas Tank Segregation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Owner --> GasTank : funds gas-payment ETH\n(FR-005 / C-004)
GasTank ..> Ethereum : pays gas for transactions
GasTank -[hidden]-> WalletNode

' ─── Owner Interactions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Owner --> WalletCore : deposit ETH / ERC20 assets\n(FR-004)
Owner --> WalletCore : wallet operations

' ─── Controller Interactions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
Controller --> ControllerLogic : service / confirmation\noperations (FR-006)

' ─── Go Binding Interactions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
Integrator --> ControllerBinding : Call (adminCount, controllerCount)\nTransact / Transfer\n(FR-006, FR-007, FR-008)
Integrator --> WalletBinding : wallet contract calls\n(FR-009)
Integrator --> GenericBinding : Call (read-only)\nTransact / Transfer (paid)\n(FR-009)

ControllerBinding --> ControllerLogic : read-only call / paid txn\n(NFR-003)
WalletBinding --> WalletCore : contract interaction
GenericBinding --> Ethereum : generic contract\nread/write operations

' ─── Legend ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
legend right
  |= Symbol |= Meaning |
  | --> | Direct invocation / data flow |
  | ..> | ENS resolution / dependency |
  | actor | External user role |
  | node  | Deployed contract or runtime unit |
  | component | Internal logic component |
  | database  | Managed data store |
  | cloud | External network infrastructure |
endlegend

@enduml
samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #333333
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "TokenCard Contracts\nHigh Level Architecture" as TokenCardContracts {
    rectangle "Proxy.sol" as ProxySol

    rectangle "Consumer Contract Wallet v3.3.1\nLibrary Contract\nwallet.sol" as WalletSol

    rectangle "controller.sol" as ControllerSol
    rectangle "tokenWhitelist.sol" as TokenWhitelistSol
    rectangle "oracle.sol" as OracleSol
    rectangle "license.sol" as LicenseSol
    rectangle "walletDeployer.sol" as WalletDeployerSol
    rectangle "walletCache.sol" as WalletCacheSol

    rectangle "Gnosis MultiSig\ncryptofloat.tokencard.eth" as GnosisMultiSig
    rectangle "TKN Holder Contract\nholder.sol" as TknHolderContract
}

ProxySol --> WalletSol : proxies
WalletSol --> ControllerSol : resolves via ENS
WalletSol --> TokenWhitelistSol : resolves via ENS\nwhitelist checks
WalletSol --> OracleSol : resolves via ENS\nexchange rates
WalletSol --> LicenseSol : resolves via ENS
LicenseSol --> GnosisMultiSig : ENS
LicenseSol --> TknHolderContract : ENS
WalletDeployerSol --> WalletSol : deploys
WalletDeployerSol --> WalletCacheSol : cache lookup
@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The diagram successfully represents all major components, actors, data entities, and interactions described in the SRS. It includes the Owner, Controller, and Integrator actors; the on-chain contracts (Wallet, Controller, Whitelist, Oracle, Licence, TKN Holder); the off-chain Go bindings; and critical concepts like the ENS and the segregated Gas Tank. It correctly respects the specified scope limitations by illustrating the *use* of managed data (e.g., from the oracle) without inventing the management flows.

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram is highly faithful to the requirements. It accurately depicts the architectural constraints and relationships. For example, the Gas Tank is correctly shown as separate from the Wallet Contract and associated with the Owner Address (NFR-002, C-004). The dependency on ENS for contract resolution is clearly modeled (FR-002). The data flow from the Whitelist and Oracle contracts to the Wallet for decision-making is also correctly shown (FR-001, FR-003).

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The diagram presents a coherent and logical architecture that is well-suited for the problem domain. The decomposition into on-chain contracts, off-chain bindings, and external actors is clear and effective. The grouping of related logic within contracts (e.g., `ControllerLogic` and `adminCount` in the `ControllerNode`) and the use of different visual stereotypes for different element types (e.g., `node`, `component`, `database`) demonstrate a rational design approach that enhances clarity.

traceability.reasoning: Traceability is a significant strength of this diagram. Many interactions are explicitly annotated with the corresponding requirement IDs (e.g., FR-001, FR-002, NFR-003). This allows a reviewer to directly map functional and non-functional requirements to specific architectural elements and flows, which is a best practice in architecture evaluation and documentation.

readability.reasoning: The diagram is exceptionally readable. It uses a clean layout, consistent symbolism, and clear labeling. The inclusion of a legend that explains the meaning of different symbols (actors, nodes, components, arrows) removes ambiguity and makes the diagram self-contained and easy for any stakeholder to understand.

samples.title: labhire / direct/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
!define RECTANGLE class

title LabHire System Architecture

skinparam componentStyle rectangle

package "User Interfaces" {
  [Student Web UI]
  [Mentor Web UI]
  [Admin Web UI]
  [Mobile App UI]
}

package "Application Layer" {
  [Project Management Service]
  [User Authentication Service]
  [Community Service]
  [Recommendation Engine]
  [Notification Service]
  [Chat Service]
  [Admin Review Service]
  [Enterprise Certification Service]
  [Search Service]
  [Profile Management Service]
}

package "Data Access Layer" {
  [Database Interface]
  [Email/SMS Gateway]
  [File Storage Interface]
}

package "External Systems" {
  [University Email Servers]
  [Enterprise Email Servers]
  [Cloud Storage]
  [Third-party Messaging APIs]
}

package "Security & Compliance" {
  [Access Control Module]
  [Encryption Module]
  [Risk Control & Audit Logs]
}

[Student Web UI] --> [Project Management Service]
[Student Web UI] --> [Community Service]
[Student Web UI] --> [Search Service]

[Student Web UI] --> [Notification Service]

[Admin Web UI] --> [User Authentication Service]

[Mobile App UI] --> [Project Management Service]

[Mobile App UI] --> [Search Service]

[Recommendation Engine] --> [Database Interface]

[Chat Service] --> [Database Interface]

[Enterprise Certification Service] --> [Database Interface]
[Search Service] --> [Database Interface]
[Profile Management Service] --> [Database Interface]

[User Authentication Service] --> [Email/SMS Gateway]
[Enterprise Certification Service] --> [Email/SMS Gateway]
[Notification Service] --> [Email/SMS Gateway]
[Notification Service] --> [Third-party Messaging APIs]
[Chat Service] --> [Third-party Messaging APIs]

[Profile Management Service] --> [File Storage Interface]

[Database Interface] --> [Access Control Module]
[Email/SMS Gateway] --> [Encryption Module]
[Chat Service] --> [Encryption Module]
[File Storage Interface] --> [Encryption Module]

[Risk Control & Audit Logs] --> [Database Interface]

[Database Interface] <--> [Cloud Storage]

[Email/SMS Gateway] <--> [University Email Servers]
[Email/SMS Gateway] <--> [Enterprise Email Servers]

note right of [Recommendation Engine]
  Uses collaborative filtering,
  tag matching, and cold-start rules
end note

note right of [Access Control Module]
  Enforces role-based permissions:
  - Students view limited info
  - Mentors see applicant resumes
  - Admins have full access
end note

note bottom of [Risk Control & Audit Logs]
  Tracks abnormal behavior,
  supports manual analysis
  and alerting
end note

completeness.reasoning: The architecture diagram covers almost all functional requirements, including project management, community, recommendations, search, chat, profile management, enterprise certification, and admin review. The external interfaces (email) are also well-represented.

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram largely respects the SRS constraints by avoiding specific technology stacks (e.g., naming generic 'Database Interface' and 'Encryption Module'). However, it introduces 'Cloud Storage' and 'Third-party Messaging APIs' for chat, which are architectural decisions not explicitly mandated by the SRS, though they are reasonable inferences.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The architecture is logically divided into standard layers (UI, Application, Data Access, External Systems, Security). Architectural Significant Requirements (ASRs) such as access control, data confidentiality, and risk control/audit are explicitly modeled as separate, cross-cutting modules.

traceability.reasoning: Component names closely match the terminology used in the SRS. The inclusion of explanatory notes (e.g., detailing recommendation algorithms and role-based access control rules) provides excellent traceability back to specific SRS requirements.

readability.reasoning: The use of PlantUML packages cleanly organizes the system into understandable tiers. The flow from user interfaces down through services to data access and external systems is clear and easy to follow without excessive crossing lines.

title DeepCode System Architecture

}

' Business Logic Layer
package "Business Layer" {

  [Problem Management] as problemModule

  [Forum Management] as forumModule
  [AI Assistant] as aiModule
  [Judging Service] as judgeModule

  [SMTP Server] as smtp
  [JudgeHost/JudgeCore] as judgeHost
}

samples.title: SmartRecipe / direct/deepseek-v3.2
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
!define RECTANGLE class
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam linetype ortho
skinparam nodesep 20
skinparam ranksep 30

title Smart Recipe System - High-Level Architecture

' ========== EXTERNAL ENTITIES ==========
rectangle "Mobile User" as User <<External Person>>
rectangle "System Administrator" as Admin <<External Person>>
rectangle "Catering Company" as Catering <<External Person>>

' ========== MOBILE CLIENT TIER ==========
package "Mobile Client Tier" {
    [Android App] as AndroidApp
    [iOS App] as iOSApp
    note right of AndroidApp
        Contains UI for:
        - Login/Registration
        - Photo Capture/Upload
        - Recipe Display
        - Health Center
        - Community Sharing
        - Recommendations
    end note
    AndroidApp - [iOSApp] : Common UI Logic
}

' ========== API GATEWAY & COMMUNICATION ==========
node "API Gateway / Load Balancer" as Gateway {
}

' ========== BACKEND SERVICE TIER ==========
package "Backend Service Tier (Spring Boot)" as Backend {
    rectangle "Authentication Service" as AuthService
    rectangle "User Profile Service" as UserService
    rectangle "Recipe Management Service" as RecipeService
    rectangle "Health Analysis Service" as HealthService
    rectangle "Community Service" as CommunityService
    rectangle "Recommendation Service" as RecService
    rectangle "Admin Management Service" as AdminService

    AuthService .u.> UserService : Manages\nCredentials
    RecipeService .d.> HealthService : Provides Data\nfor Analysis
    CommunityService .d.> RecService : Provides Sharing Data
    AdminService .r.> CommunityService : Reviews Content
    AdminService .r.> RecipeService : Manages Recipes
}

' ========== AI/ML SERVICE TIER ==========
node "AI/ML Inference Service (Flask)" as MLService {
    rectangle "Image Preprocessing" as Preprocess
    rectangle "Food Recognition Model" as RecognitionModel
    rectangle "Recipe Generator" as RecipeGen
    Preprocess -> RecognitionModel : Image Vectors
    RecognitionModel -> RecipeGen : Food Labels/Features
}

' ========== DATA TIER ==========
package "Data Tier" {
    database "MySQL Database" as MySQL {
        folder "Structured Data" {
            [User Accounts]
            [Recipes & Ingredients]
            [Nutrition Data]
            [Health Profiles]
            [Community Posts]
            [Merchant Info]
            [Food Taboos]
            [User History]
        }
    }

    database "Redis Cache" as Redis {
        folder "Cached Data" {
            [Session Tokens]
            [Hot Recipes]
            [Recommendation Features]
            [API Responses]
        }
    }
}

' ========== EXTERNAL CONNECTIONS ==========
User --> AndroidApp : Uses
User --> iOSApp : Uses
Admin --> AndroidApp : Uses (Admin UI)
Catering .[hidden].> Admin : (Data Access via Admin)

AndroidApp --> Gateway : Ajax / Direct Calls
iOSApp --> Gateway : Ajax / Direct Calls

Gateway --> AuthService : Routes Requests
Gateway --> UserService
Gateway --> RecipeService
Gateway --> HealthService
Gateway --> CommunityService
Gateway --> RecService
Gateway --> AdminService
Gateway --> MLService : For Image Recognition

' ========== SERVICE TO DATA CONNECTIONS ==========
AuthService --> Redis : Caches Tokens
AuthService --> MySQL : Validates Credentials

UserService --> MySQL : CRUD User Profiles

RecipeService --> MySQL : CRUD Recipes
RecipeService --> Redis : Caches Recipe Data
RecipeService --> MLService : Submits Images for Recognition

HealthService --> MySQL : Reads Nutrition & Health Data
HealthService --> Redis : Caches Analysis Results

CommunityService --> MySQL : CRUD Posts, Likes, Comments
CommunityService --> Redis : Caches Feed

RecService --> MySQL : Reads User History
RecService --> Redis : Stores Features & Recommendations

AdminService --> MySQL : Manages All Data

MLService --> MySQL : Fetches Recipe DB for Generation
MLService --> Redis : (Optional) Caches Model Results

' ========== DATA FLOW NOTES ==========
note top of MLService
    **Image Recognition Flow:**
    1. Receive image from client
    2. Preprocess & validate
    3. Run inference model
    4. Match with recipe DB
    5. Return recipe data
end note

note bottom of MySQL
    **Stores:**
    - User data (DR-001, DR-002)
    - Recipes (DR-004)
    - Health info (DR-005, DR-006)
    - Ingredients & Taboos (DR-012, DR-013)
    - Community data (DR-009, DR-010, DR-011)
    - History (DR-014)
end note

note left of Redis
    **Performance Cache:**
    - Session management
    - Frequently accessed data
    - Recommendation features
    (NFR-004)
end note

@enduml

samples.pred_source:
@startuml

package "CLI Layer" {
    component "CLI Entry Point" as cli_entry
    component "Trace Command" as trace_cmd
    component "Event Registration Structure" as event_reg
}

package "Library/Loader Layer" {
    component "Probe Loader" as probe_loader
    component "Userspace Tracer" as userspace_tracer
    component "gobpf Library" as gobpf
}

package "Configuration Generator" {
    component "Configuration Generator" as config_gen
    database "Event Configuration Metadata" as event_config
}

package "Kernel eBPF Layer" {
    component "Main eBPF Program" as main_ebpf
    component "Handler eBPF Programs" as handler_ebpf
    database "Program Maps (handle_NAME_progs)" as prog_maps
    database "Return-Program Maps (handle_NAME_progs_ret)" as ret_maps
    component "No-op Handler" as noop_handler
}

package "Event Channel" {
    component "Perf Event Channel" as perf_channel
    component "Common Event Header" as event_header
}

rectangle "Linux Kernel" as linux_kernel {
    component "eBPF/kprobe facilities" as ebpf_facilities
    component "kprobe/handle_NAME" as kprobe_attach
    component "kretprobe/handle_NAME" as kretprobe_attach
}

' Dependencies within the system
cli_entry --> trace_cmd : "executes"
trace_cmd --> event_reg : "parses CLI args into"
trace_cmd --> probe_loader : "initiates tracing"
event_reg --> probe_loader : "provides registration data"
probe_loader --> gobpf : "uses"
gobpf --> linux_kernel : "interfaces with"
probe_loader --> main_ebpf : "loads"
probe_loader --> handler_ebpf : "loads"
probe_loader --> noop_handler : "loads for unconfigured probes"
config_gen --> event_config : "reads"
config_gen --> handler_ebpf : "generates source code for"
main_ebpf --> prog_maps : "selects default handlers from"
main_ebpf --> ret_maps : "selects return handlers from"
prog_maps --> handler_ebpf : "contains"
ret_maps --> handler_ebpf : "contains"
handler_ebpf --> event_header : "prepends to events"
handler_ebpf --> perf_channel : "emits events to"
noop_handler --> perf_channel : "emits no events"
perf_channel --> userspace_tracer : "transfers events to"
userspace_tracer --> probe_loader : "coordinates with"
kprobe_attach --> handler_ebpf : "attaches"
kretprobe_attach --> handler_ebpf : "attaches"

' External dependencies
main_ebpf --> linux_kernel : "runs in"
handler_ebpf --> linux_kernel : "runs in"
noop_handler --> linux_kernel : "runs in"

@enduml
samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #34495E
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "Linux Kernel Functions" as KernelFunctions {
    rectangle "sys_write()" as SysWrite
    rectangle "sys_open()" as SysOpen
    rectangle "inet_csk_accept()" as InetAccept
}

package "Main eBPF Program" as MainEbpf {
    rectangle "Kprobe\nentry probe" as Kprobe
    rectangle "Kretprobe\nreturn probe" as Kretprobe
    rectangle "Kprobe / Kretprobe\nother traced calls" as OtherProbe
    database "Prog Map\npid to fd" as ProgMap
    database "Ret Prog Map\npid to fd" as RetProgMap
    database "Prog Maps\nhandler mappings" as OtherProgMaps
}

database "Prog ID Map\npid to id" as ProgIdMap

package "Event Handler eBPF Programs" as HandlerPrograms {
    rectangle "Handler Probe\nhandle_syscall_write" as WriteHandler
    rectangle "Default Handler Probes" as DefaultHandlers
    rectangle "Process Specific\nHandler Probes" as ProcessHandlers
}

database "Perf Event Map\nshared event map" as PerfEventMap

rectangle "TraceLeft\nCLI and library tracer" as TraceLeft

SysWrite --> Kprobe : triggers entry probe
SysWrite --> Kretprobe : triggers return probe
SysOpen --> OtherProbe : triggers trace probe
InetAccept --> OtherProbe : triggers network trace

Kprobe --> ProgMap : lookup handler fd
Kretprobe --> RetProgMap : lookup return handler fd
OtherProbe --> OtherProgMaps : lookup handler mapping

TraceLeft --> ProgMap : update default handlers
TraceLeft --> RetProgMap : update return handlers
TraceLeft --> OtherProgMaps : update process handlers
TraceLeft --> ProgIdMap : update pid to program id

ProgMap --> WriteHandler : tail call
RetProgMap --> WriteHandler : tail call return handler
OtherProgMaps --> DefaultHandlers : tail call
OtherProgMaps --> ProcessHandlers : tail call
WriteHandler --> ProgIdMap : lookup program id

WriteHandler --> PerfEventMap : perf_event_output\ncommon section first
DefaultHandlers --> PerfEventMap : emit events\ncommon section first
ProcessHandlers --> PerfEventMap : emit events\ncommon section first
PerfEventMap --> TraceLeft : events
@enduml

@startuml
title EIVAS Web Application - System Architecture (Component View)
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false
left to right direction

actor "Epidemiological\nForm Filler" as Filler
actor "Epidemiological\nAnalyst" as Analyst

package "Presentation Layer (Web UI)" {
  component "Reporting Form UI" <<UI>> as UI_Report
  component "Case Management UI" <<UI>> as UI_Case
  component "Map Display UI" <<UI>> as UI_Map
  component "Association Display UI" <<UI>> as UI_Assoc
  component "Relationship Graph UI" <<UI>> as UI_Graph
  component "Statistical Charts UI" <<UI>> as UI_Charts
}

package "Application Layer" {
  component "Backend API Service" <<Service>> as API
  component "Data Quality Validator" <<Service>> as DQV
  component "Rate Limiter" <<Service>> as RL
  component "Encryption Module" <<Service>> as ENC
}

package "Analytics" {
  component "Algorithm Module\n(risk, clustering)" <<Service>> as Algo
}

package "Data Layer" {
  database "Back-end Database" <<Database>> as DB
}

package "External Services" {
  component "Map/Geo Service" <<External>> as MAP
}

Filler --> UI_Report : enter/edit investigation info\n(FR-001, FR-004)
Analyst --> UI_Case : manage and query cases\n(FR-005..FR-008)
Analyst --> UI_Map : view itineraries\n(FR-009)
Analyst --> UI_Assoc : find associated cases\n(FR-010)
Analyst --> UI_Graph : case relationship graph\n(FR-011)
Analyst --> UI_Charts : flow maps, clusters, heatmap,\ntrends, distributions (FR-012..FR-018)

UI_Report --> API : submit investigation records
UI_Case --> API : query/search/sort/filter cases
UI_Map --> API : fetch itinerary paths
UI_Assoc --> API : query associations
UI_Graph --> API : fetch relationships
UI_Charts --> API : request analytics outputs

API --> DB : store/retrieve cases,\nitineraries, associations
API --> Algo : provide case data;\nrequest risk/clusters
Algo --> API : computed analytics results
API --> DB : persist analytics metadata\nand data quality state

UI_Map ..> MAP : map tiles/geocoding\n(AD-003)

API --> DQV : check completeness/validity\nbefore analysis
DQV --> API : errors and management prompts\nwhen data insufficient

API --> RL : enforce request cooldown\n(NFR-005)
API --> ENC : encrypt personal info queries\n(NFR-004)

note right of RL
Cooldown frequent querying IPs
to protect resources (NFR-005)
end note

note right of ENC
Personal information query traffic must
be encrypted (NFR-004)
end note

note bottom of API
Robustness (NFR-001), 100-user <=1s performance (NFR-002),
browser/OS compatibility (NFR-006), maintainability and usability
targets (NFR-007..NFR-009)
end note

component "Backup Backend (Standby)" <<Service>> as Backup
API .. Backup : failover within 20 minutes (NFR-003)

@enduml

samples.title: s000024_99ab1380 / mini-swe-agent/claude-sonnet-4-6
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
title OCI Jenkins Terraform Deployment - Architecture

skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam backgroundColor white
skinparam defaultFontSize 12

actor "Deployment Operator" as operator

package "Deployment Workflow Layer" {
    component "Terraform CLI" as terraform_cli
    component "terraform.tfvars\n(Variable Files)" as tfvars
    component "JSON Input Files" as json_input
    component "Terraform Modules" as tf_modules {
        component "Root Module\n(OCI + SSH + Cluster Config)" as root_module
        component "Jenkins Master Module" as master_module
        component "Jenkins Slave/Agent Module" as slave_module
    }
}

package "OCI Infrastructure Layer" {
    package "OCI Tenancy / Compartment" {
        package "Virtual Cloud Network (VCN)" {
            package "Public Subnet" {
                component "Jenkins Master Instance\n(Compute VM)" as jenkins_master
                component "Load Balancer\n(optional public entry)" as load_balancer
            }
            package "Private Subnet(s)" {
                component "Jenkins Slave/Agent\nInstances (Compute VMs)" as jenkins_slaves
                component "NAT Gateway\n(Outbound Connectivity)" as nat_gateway
            }
            component "Bastion Host\n(Controlled SSH Access)" as bastion_host
        }
        database "OCI Identity / Credentials\n(tenancy_ocid, user_ocid,\ncompartment_ocid, fingerprint)" as oci_identity
        component "OCI Networking Objects\n(Route Tables, DHCP, Security Lists,\nSubnets, VNICs)" as oci_network_objects
    }
}

package "Configuration & Security Layer" {
    component "SSH Key Material\n(ssh_authorized_keys,\nssh_private_key)" as ssh_keys
    component "Cloud-Init Data\n(base64-encoded)" as cloud_init
    component "Jenkins Cluster Config\n(label_prefix, version,\npassword, http_port, jnlp_port,\nplugins)" as jenkins_config
}

' Operator interactions
operator --> terraform_cli : runs terraform init/plan/apply
operator --> tfvars : edits variable files
operator --> json_input : provides example inputs

' Workflow layer connections
tfvars --> terraform_cli : provides variables
json_input --> terraform_cli : provides test inputs
terraform_cli --> tf_modules : executes modules
root_module --> master_module : passes master config
root_module --> slave_module : passes slave/agent config

' Configuration inputs
ssh_keys --> root_module
cloud_init --> master_module
cloud_init --> slave_module
jenkins_config --> root_module
oci_identity --> root_module

' Module to OCI provisioning
master_module --> jenkins_master : provisions
slave_module --> jenkins_slaves : provisions (slave_count)

' OCI infrastructure dependencies
jenkins_master --> oci_network_objects : attached via VNIC
jenkins_slaves --> oci_network_objects : attached via VNIC (private subnets)
jenkins_slaves --> nat_gateway : outbound connectivity (NAT)
oci_network_objects --> oci_identity : scoped to tenancy/compartment

' Jenkins communication
jenkins_master --> jenkins_slaves : Jenkins Master-to-Slave\n(HTTP port / JNLP port)

' Access paths
bastion_host --> jenkins_master : SSH bastion-mediated access
bastion_host --> jenkins_slaves : SSH bastion-mediated access
load_balancer --> jenkins_master : public HTTP/HTTPS entry
operator ..> bastion_host : SSH (bastion_host,\nbastion_user,\nbastion_private_key)

@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The diagram is exceptionally complete, representing all major architectural elements from the requirements. It includes the operator actor, the Terraform workflow components (CLI, variables, modules), detailed OCI infrastructure (VCN, public/private subnets, instances), and specific prerequisites for private networking like the NAT Gateway, Bastion Host, and Load Balancer. All key configuration artifacts (SSH keys, Cloud-Init, cluster config) are also explicitly shown.

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram accurately represents the relationships and interactions described in the SRS. The provisioning flow from the operator to Terraform and then to OCI is correct. The network topology, including the placement of the master in a public subnet and slaves in private subnets with NAT for egress, faithfully models the requirements for both standard and private deployments (NFR-005, FR-010).

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The architecture demonstrates strong rationality by directly addressing key requirements and quality attributes. The separation of configuration (`tfvars`, `jenkins_config`) from provisioning logic (`tf_modules`) supports modifiability (NFR-006). The explicit private network topology with controlled access points (Bastion, Load Balancer) directly addresses security and network exposure control requirements (NFR-005). The modular structure is a sound architectural choice.

traceability.reasoning: Traceability is excellent. Individual requirements can be easily mapped to specific components and relationships in the diagram. For example, FR-011 (Support Bastion Host Inputs) is not only represented by a 'Bastion Host' component but the connection from the operator is explicitly annotated with the required variables (`bastion_host`, `bastion_user`). This level of detail makes it simple to verify coverage.

readability.reasoning: The diagram is highly readable due to its clear layout, logical grouping of components into packages (e.g., 'Deployment Workflow Layer', 'Public Subnet'), and consistent notation. Labels are concise yet descriptive, and the flow of control and data is easy to follow, making the complex architecture understandable at a glance.

samples.title: easylatex / mini-swe-agent/gpt-5
samples.pred_source:
﻿@startuml
left to right direction
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam packageStyle rectangle

actor User

package Presentation_Layer {
  [Browser Frontend SPA] as Frontend
}

package Application_Layer {
  [Web Server / API Gateway] as Gateway
  [Backend API Service Python] as Backend
  [Realtime Gateway WebSocket] as Realtime
}

package Processing_Layer {
  [Auth & Permission Service] as Auth
  [File Ingestion Service] as Ingest
  [Recognition Service OCR + DL] as Recognizer
  [LaTeX Generation Service] as TexGen
  [Rendering Service] as Renderer
  [Project & Workspace Service] as Projects
  [Collaboration & Version Control] as Collab
  [Export Service] as Exporter
}

package Data_Layer {
  [Relational Database] as DB
  [File/Object Storage] as Storage
}

package External_Dependencies {
  [LaTeX Engine TeX Live / MiKTeX] as TeXEngine
}

User --> Frontend : UI interactions
Frontend --> Gateway : HTTPS REST
Frontend ..> Realtime : WebSocket
Gateway --> Backend
Gateway --> Realtime

Backend --> Auth
Backend --> Ingest
Backend --> Projects
Backend --> Collab
Backend --> Recognizer
Backend --> Exporter
Backend --> DB : Users, sessions

Recognizer --> TexGen
TexGen --> Renderer
Renderer --> Storage : Preview/Artifacts

Auth --> DB
Projects --> DB
Collab --> DB
Ingest --> Storage
Exporter --> Storage : Exports

Renderer --> TeXEngine

@enduml

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The system is designed with a logical layered architecture (Presentation, Application, Processing, Data, External). samples.title: s000007_6883c7be / metagpt-custom/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct
samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
top to bottom direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #111111
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BorderColor #111111
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #111111
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "Clients" as Clients {
    rectangle "Web Client" as WebClient
    rectangle "APP Client" as AppClient
    rectangle "Business Server" as BusinessClient
    rectangle "Developer" as Developer
}

package "Access Layer\nKeepalived(VIP)" as AccessLayer {
    rectangle "Openresty" as OpenrestyA
    rectangle "Openresty" as OpenrestyB
}

package "Proxy Layer" as ProxyLayer {
    rectangle "cgi_proxy 2" as CgiProxy2

    package "cgi_proxy 1" as CgiProxy1 {
        rectangle "Logging" as Logging
        rectangle "Appkey\nValidate" as AppkeyValidate
        rectangle "Global Limit" as GlobalLimit
        rectangle "Login &\nPermission" as LoginPermission
        rectangle "Proxy Core" as ProxyCore
        rectangle "Formatter" as Formatter

        package "Plugin" as Plugin {
            rectangle "Attach Check" as AttachCheck
            rectangle "Download" as Download
            rectangle "Upload" as Upload
            rectangle "Mock" as Mock
            rectangle "Check\nParameter" as CheckParameter
            rectangle "Agent" as Agent
        }
    }
}

package "Support Platform" as SupportPlatform {
    rectangle "Redis Cache" as RedisCache
    database "Mysql" as Mysql
    queue "Msg Center\nKafka" as MsgCenter
    database "Data Bank\nHDFS/Hbase/Druid" as DataBank
    rectangle "User Center" as UserCenter
    rectangle "Permission\nCenter" as PermissionCenter
}

package "CGI Portal" as CgiPortal {
    rectangle "CGI Manager" as CgiManager
    rectangle "Metrics &\nReport" as MetricsReport
    rectangle "Case Manager" as CaseManager
    rectangle "Log & Tree\ninquire" as LogTreeInquire
    rectangle "Appkey\nManager" as AppkeyManager
    rectangle "Http Test\nClient" as HttpTestClient
    rectangle "Service\nManager" as ServiceManager
    rectangle "Document\nGenerate" as DocumentGenerate
}

rectangle "Log consumer" as LogConsumer

package "Task Scheduler" as TaskScheduler {
    rectangle "Log Analysis" as LogAnalysis
    rectangle "CGI Case\nExecutor" as CgiCaseExecutor
    rectangle "Monitor/Alarm" as MonitorAlarm
    rectangle "CGI Health\nCheck" as CgiHealthCheck
    rectangle "Daily Report" as DailyReport
}

package "Business Servers" as BusinessServers {
    rectangle "Business Server A" as BusinessServerA
    rectangle "Business Server B" as BusinessServerB
    rectangle "Business Server C" as BusinessServerC
    rectangle "......" as OtherBusinessServers
}

WebClient --> AccessLayer : Request / Response
AppClient --> AccessLayer : Request / Response
BusinessClient --> AccessLayer : Request / Response
AccessLayer --> CgiProxy1 : route
AccessLayer --> CgiProxy2 : route

Formatter --> ProxyCore
AppkeyValidate --> RedisCache : appkey cache
AppkeyValidate --> Mysql : appkey metadata
GlobalLimit --> RedisCache : rate limit
LoginPermission --> UserCenter : authenticate
LoginPermission --> PermissionCenter : authorize
Logging --> MsgCenter : logs
ProxyCore ..> Plugin : plugin hooks
Upload --> DataBank : upload data
Download --> DataBank : download data
Agent --> BusinessServers : invoke backend

Developer --> CgiPortal
CgiPortal --> Mysql : manage config/cases
CgiPortal --> DataBank : query logs/metrics/docs
HttpTestClient --> AccessLayer : test request

MsgCenter --> LogConsumer : log stream
LogConsumer --> LogAnalysis
LogConsumer --> DailyReport
LogAnalysis --> DataBank : analytics data
DailyReport --> DataBank : report data
CgiCaseExecutor --> BusinessServers : execute cases
CgiHealthCheck --> BusinessServers : health check
@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The diagram identifies most of the primary components required by the SRS, such as the HTTP Proxy, Test Server, Test Client, and Upstream Server. It also correctly includes the Local Filesystem and the Grizzly/Jersey environment. However, it fails to represent key non-functional requirements and constraints that are architecturally significant, such as the multi-module build structure (NFR-004), the test client's executor service and shutdown logic (NFR-003, C-003), or the debug logging capabilities (NFR-002).

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram contains several significant misrepresentations of the system's control and data flow described in the SRS. For instance, the relationship 'Async HTTP Client --> Grizzly/Jersey HTTP Server' is incorrect; the client should target the Test Server itself. The relationship 'Structured Response Builder --> Test Client' is inverted; the server builds the response, which the client receives. Similarly, 'Upload Endpoint --> Test Server' is a backwards representation of composition. The 'Test Server --> Upstream Server' interaction is not specified in the requirements and confuses the roles of the components.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The diagram introduces several components that are unsupported implementation-level inferences rather than required architectural elements. The 'Request Queue' is the most significant; while the SRS requires retaining a request (FR-002), specifying a 'Queue' is a specific design choice not mandated by the requirements. The 'Channel Manager' and 'Structured Response Builder' are also inferred implementation patterns, not explicit architectural components from the SRS. These unsupported inferences make the diagram a proposed design rather than a faithful architectural representation of the requirements.

traceability.reasoning: Several diagram elements can be traced back to specific requirements. The proxy forwarding logic (FR-002, C-002) is represented by the 'HTTP Proxy' and its interaction with the 'Channel Manager'. The file handling endpoints (FR-004, API-003) and their dependency on the filesystem (C-004) are also present. However, traceability is significantly weakened by the unsupported components ('Request Queue') and incorrect relationships. It is impossible to correctly trace the structured response flow (DEF-003) or the file upload response map (FR-007) through the inverted connectors in the diagram.

readability.reasoning: The diagram is structured with logical groupings (layers) and uses standard component notation, which provides a basic level of clarity. However, the numerous incorrect and inverted relationships make the actual control and data flows difficult to understand correctly. An architect reading this diagram would be misled about how components interact, for example, believing the Test Server communicates with the Upstream Server or that the client pulls data from a response builder. The awkward separation of a component ('Test Server') from its runtime ('Grizzly/Jersey HTTP Server') into different parent nodes also adds confusion.

samples.pred_source:
@startuml
!define RECTANGLE class

RECTANGLE "Authenticated User" as user
RECTANGLE "Keycloak" as keycloak
RECTANGLE "API Gateway" as gateway
RECTANGLE "Example Runtime Bundle" as runtimeBundle
RECTANGLE "ProcessRuntime API" as processRuntime
RECTANGLE "TaskRuntime API" as taskRuntime
RECTANGLE "Audit Service" as auditService
RECTANGLE "Query Service" as queryService
RECTANGLE "NGINX Ingress Controller" as ingress
RECTANGLE "Kubernetes/GKE Cluster" as kubernetes
RECTANGLE "Deployment Descriptors" as deploymentDescriptors

' Main flows
user --> keycloak : Obtain authentication token
user --> gateway : Access protected endpoints\n(with token)
gateway --> runtimeBundle : Route requests
runtimeBundle --> processRuntime : Execute process operations
runtimeBundle --> taskRuntime : Execute task operations
runtimeBundle --> auditService : Log audit events
runtimeBundle --> queryService : Provide query data
ingress --> kubernetes : Route external traffic
kubernetes --> runtimeBundle : Host services
deploymentDescriptors --> kubernetes : Configure scalability

' Security and auth dependencies
gateway ..> keycloak : Validate tokens
taskRuntime ..> keycloak : Check user roles
processRuntime ..> keycloak : Validate access

' Background components (dotted connections indicate architectural context)
RECTANGLE "Notification Service" as notificationService
RECTANGLE "Cloud Connectors" as cloudConnectors
RECTANGLE "Registry/Config Maps" as registry

notificationService ..> runtimeBundle : (background)
cloudConnectors ..> runtimeBundle : (background)
registry ..> kubernetes : (background)

note right of user
  - Authenticated user obtains token from Keycloak
  - Uses token to access protected endpoints via Gateway
end note

note right of runtimeBundle
  - Hosts process definitions and instances
  - Contains ProcessRuntime and TaskRuntime APIs
  - Generates audit events
  - Provides query capabilities
end note

note bottom of kubernetes
  - Hosts all cloud services
  - Configured via deployment descriptors
  - Exposes services through NGINX ingress
end note

@enduml

left to right direction

}

rectangle "Client" as Client

    rectangle "Gateway" as Gateway
    rectangle "Registry\n(Kubernetes)" as Registry
    rectangle "Config Maps\n(Kubernetes)" as ConfigMaps

        }

        package "Runtime Bundle" as RuntimeBundle {

            rectangle "BPMN, Form\nand Rule Defs" as BpmnFormRuleDefs
        }
    }
}

Keycloak --> Client : Direct Access Grant
Gateway --> Keycloak : WebFlow
Gateway --> CloudApplication : routed service access
Gateway --> Registry : service discovery
Registry --> ConfigMaps : configuration lookup
ConfigMaps --> CloudConnectors : Kubernetes configuration
QueryService --> RuntimeBundle : process queries
AuditService --> RuntimeBundle : audit events
NotificationService --> RuntimeBundle : notifications
CloudConnectors --> RuntimeBundle : business integration
ProcessRuntime --> BpmnFormRuleDefs : executes definitions
RuntimeBundle --> Keycloak : identity and token validation
@enduml

@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false

title AwesomeMusic - System Architecture

rectangle "User (Browser)" as user

package "Web Layer" {
  component "Web Frontend\n(HTML/CSS/JS/Bootstrap)" as FE
  component "API Gateway\nNginx 1.19.8" as NGINX
}

package "Backend Layer" {
  component "Web Backend\n(Golang + Gin)" as BE
}

package "Algorithm Layer" {
  component "Algorithm Service" as ALG
}

package "Scheduling" {
  component "Scheduling Module\n(02:00 daily + on-demand)" as SCH
}

package "Infrastructure" {
  database "Relational DB\n(Accounts, favorites, follows,\nscore metadata, hot charts,\nmodel metadata)" as DB
  component "Hadoop 3.2.2\n(Score/MIDI storage)" as HDFS
  component "RabbitMQ 3.8.14\n(Task Queue)" as MQ
  component "etcd 3.4.15\n(Registry/Config)" as ETCD
}

' Key interactions
user --> FE : HTTPS
FE --> NGINX : HTTPS
NGINX --> BE : HTTP/TCP

BE --> DB
BE --> HDFS
BE --> ETCD

' Task orchestration
BE --> MQ : Enqueue generation\nand other jobs
SCH --> MQ : Enqueue daily\ntrain/classify/recommend
SCH --> ETCD : Config/coordination

ALG --> MQ : Consume tasks\n+ publish status
ALG --> HDFS : Read/Write\nmidi/abc/scores
ALG --> DB : Update labels,\nmodels,recommendations

' Direct request path for recommendations
BE --> ALG : Recommend API\n(on-demand)

note right of ALG
- Online generation: midi->abc, seed extraction, generate, abc->midi
- Daily training: user-specific GPT-2
- Classification: score labeling
- Recommendation: user/song similarity
end note

samples.pred_source:
@startuml
' Refined Layered Component/Deployment Diagram for Model Evolution Version Management Visualization Tool

' Presentation Layer
node "Presentation Layer" {
    component "Browser Client" as browser
    component "Frontend Module" as frontend
    component "Account Management UI" as accountUI
    component "Model Search & Viewing UI" as searchUI
    component "Model Upload & Editing UI" as uploadUI
    component "Evolution Relationship Editing UI" as evolutionUI
    component "User Management UI" as userUI
}

' Application Layer
node "Application Layer" {
    component "Backend Module" as backend
    component "Security Module" as security
    component "Encryption Module" as encryption
    component "Access Control Module" as accessControl
    component "Algorithm Support Module" as algoModule
}

' Data Layer
node "Data Layer" {
    database "Neo4j Graph Database" as neo4j
    database "MySQL Relational Database" as mysql
}

' External Systems
cloud "External Systems" {
    component "Python Libraries" as pythonLibs
    component "Console Interface" as console
}

' Dependencies
browser --> frontend : HTTPS
frontend --> backend : HTTPS
backend --> neo4j : Bolt Protocol
backend --> mysql : JDBC over TLS
backend --> algoModule : Function Calls
algoModule --> pythonLibs : Library Import
frontend --> algoModule : HTTPS (AD-003)
security --> backend : OAuth 2.0 Integration
encryption --> backend : TLS 1.3 / AES-256
accessControl --> backend : Policy Enforcement
console --> backend : UDP / SMTP

@enduml

@startuml
' Directional setting (Top to Down)
top to bottom direction

' Styling for different layers
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor<<Frontend>> #e1f5fe
    BorderColor<<Frontend>> #01579b
    
    BackgroundColor<<Presentation>> #f3e5f5
    BorderColor<<Presentation>> #4a148c
    
    BackgroundColor<<Backend>> #fff3e0
    BorderColor<<Backend>> #e65100
}

skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor<<Storage>> #e8f5e9
    BorderColor<<Storage>> #1b5e20
}

    rectangle "React Framework" as React <<Frontend>>

}

package "Data Presentation Layer" {

}

package "Backend Layer" {
    rectangle "Flask: Backend Framework" as Flask <<Backend>>
    rectangle "SQLAlchemy: ORM Framework" as SQLAlchemy <<Backend>>
    rectangle "Netron: Model Conversion & Parsing" as NetronBE <<Backend>>
}

package "Storage / Database Layer" {
    database "MySQL: Relational Database" as MySQL <<Storage>>
    database "Neo4j: Graph Database" as Neo4j <<Storage>>
}

React --> Router
React --> NetronFE
React --> RESTAPI
NetronFE --> GraphLang
RESTAPI --> Flask
GraphLang --> Flask
Flask --> SQLAlchemy
Flask --> NetronBE
SQLAlchemy --> MySQL
Flask --> Neo4j

  node "App VM(s)\nUbuntu 18.04" as app {
    component backA as "Web Backend API\n(Golang/Gin) - Instance A"
    component backB as "Web Backend API\n(Golang/Gin) - Instance B"
    component sched as "Scheduler\n- Daily 02:00 tasks (FR-016)\n- User-submitted generation tasks (FR-017)\n- Poll task status & retries\n- Log errors"
  }

  node "AI VM(s)\nUbuntu 18.04" as ai {
    component genw as "Algorithm Service\nGeneration Worker (FR-013)"
    component trainw as "Algorithm Service\nTraining Worker (FR-012)"
    component classw as "Algorithm Service\nClassification Worker (FR-014)"
    component recow as "Algorithm Service\nRecommendation Worker (FR-015)"
  }

  node "Infrastructure VM(s)\nUbuntu 18.04" as infra {
    component mq as "RabbitMQ 3.8.14\nTask Queue (MQ)"
    component etcd as "etcd 3.4.15\nConfig / Coordination"
    node hdfs as "Hadoop 3.2.2\nHDFS: Scores (abc), MIDI, Models"
    database db as "Metadata Store\nUser, Scores, Favorites, Follows,\nModel favorites, Recommendations,\nHot charts, Task status, Logs"
  }
}

' External access and edge
user --> nginx : HTTPS (UI, API, Download)
nginx --> fe : Serve static assets
user <-- nginx : HTML/JS/CSS
nginx --> backA : Reverse proxy API (HTTPS/TCP)
nginx --> backB : Reverse proxy API (HTTPS/TCP)

' Backend to storage and infra
backA --> db : CRUD\n(Accounts, scores, relations,\nreco lists, hot charts,\ntask status, logs)
backB --> db : CRUD
backA --> hdfs : File I/O\n(abc, MIDI, generated scores,\nmodel artifacts)
backB --> hdfs : File I/O
backA --> mq : Enqueue tasks / Poll status
backB --> mq : Enqueue tasks / Poll status
sched --> mq : Enqueue daily batches\n(training, classification,\nrecommendation) at 02:00

' Algorithm workers and data flows
mq --> genw : Generation tasks\n(seed, model ID)
mq --> trainw : Training tasks\n(user IDs, playlists)
mq --> classw : Classification tasks\n(score IDs)
mq --> recow : Recommendation tasks\n(user IDs, song lists)

genw --> hdfs : Read seed (abc/MIDI)\nRead/Write models\nWrite generated MIDI/abc
trainw --> hdfs : Read playlists (MIDI/abc)\nWrite updated user models
classw --> hdfs : Read scores (abc/MIDI)\nWrite score labels
recow --> hdfs : Read score representations\nWrite recommendation results

genw --> mq : Status / Results\n(success, retries, exceptions)
trainw --> mq : Status
classw --> mq : Status
recow --> mq : Status

' Coordination/config
backA --> etcd : Config / Service registry
backB --> etcd : Config / Service registry
genw --> etcd : Config / Coordination
trainw --> etcd : Config / Coordination
classw --> etcd : Config / Coordination
recow --> etcd : Config / Coordination

' Notes mapping requirements and qualities
note right of nginx
- Isolates external/internal boundary (NFR-020)
- Load balances Backend instances (NFR-025)
- Serves static Web Frontend
end note

note bottom of app
Backend responsibilities:
- Accounts & Auth (FR-001, FR-002)
- Favorites & Follows (FR-003, FR-004, FR-007)
- Score Upload/Download/Search (FR-006, FR-009, FR-011)
- Recommendations & Hot charts (FR-008, FR-010, FR-018)
- Web-side score generation orchestration (FR-005)
Non-functional targets:
- Page response <= 1s (C-003)
- >=2000 QPS (NFR-016), >=100 TPS (NFR-017)
end note

note bottom of ai
Algorithm service:
- Daily model training per user (FR-012)
- Online score generation with retries (FR-013)
- Daily classification and recommendation (FR-014, FR-015)
Performance:
- Training <= 1.5h avg (NFR-012, C-004)
- Generation <= 1min avg (NFR-013, C-007)
- Daily classification/reco <= 1.5h avg (NFR-014, NFR-015)
end note

note top of infra
Infrastructure:
- RabbitMQ for reliable task delivery (FR-016, FR-017)
- Hadoop HDFS for files/models (abc, MIDI, params)
- etcd for coordination/config
- Metadata Store for structured data and task logs (DR-001..DR-016)
Replication & availability:
- Important data 3 replicas (NFR-021)
- Survive partial failures (NFR-022)
end note

legend left
Modules:
- Web Frontend (HTML/CSS/JS/Bootstrap)
- Web Backend (Golang/Gin) [scalable instances]
- Scheduler (batch & on-demand tasks)
- Algorithm Service (workers for gen/train/classify/reco)
- Infrastructure: Nginx, RabbitMQ, etcd, Hadoop HDFS, Metadata Store
Protocols:
- HTTPS and TCP/IP (C-012)
endlegend

@enduml

samples.title: ModelSharePlatform / metagpt-custom/deepseek-v3.2
samples.pred_source:
@startuml

package "Presentation Layer" {
    component "User Interface Component" as UI
    component "Internationalization Component" as I18N
}

package "Application / Business Logic Layer" {
    component "Authentication &\nAuthorization Component" as Auth
    component "Content Management Component" as Content
    component "Model Management Component" as Model {
        component "Model Upload &\nReview" as ModelUpload
        component "Online Model Invocation" as ModelInvocation
    }
    component "Dataset Management Component" as Dataset
    component "Follow Management Component" as Follow
    component "Administration Component" as Admin
}

package "Data Access Layer" {
    component "Data Persistence Component" as Persistence
    component "File Storage Service" as FileStorage
}

package "Data Storage Layer" {
    database "Database" as DB
}

rectangle "External Systems" {
    cloud "Email Service" as Email
    cloud "Payment Gateway" as Gateway
}

' Key Dependencies
Auth --> UI : "protects access"
UI --> Content : "creates/views content"
UI --> Model : "manages models"
UI --> Dataset : "manages datasets"
UI --> Follow : "follows/unfollows users"
UI --> Admin : "administrative actions"
I18N --> UI : "provides localized strings"

Content --> Persistence : "stores/retrieves\nQ&A & blog data"
ModelUpload --> Persistence : "stores/retrieves\nmodel metadata"
ModelUpload --> FileStorage : "stores/serves\nmodel files"
ModelInvocation --> Persistence : "reads model\nconfiguration"
Dataset --> Persistence : "stores/retrieves\ndataset metadata"
Dataset --> FileStorage : "stores/serves\ndataset files"
Follow --> Persistence : "stores/retrieves\nfollow relationships"
Admin --> Content : "executes content\ndeletions"
Admin --> Model : "executes model\ndeletions"
Admin --> Auth : "manages user\naccounts"

Persistence --> DB : "CRUD operations"
FileStorage --> DB : "stores/retrieves\nfile metadata"

' External Dependencies
Auth --> Email : "sends password\nrecovery emails"
UI --> Gateway : "initiates payment"

@enduml

@startuml
' --- Configuration & Styles ---
skinparam topboxSelection true
skinparam Direction top to bottom
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false

' Define Styles based on original ClassDefs
<style>
    .client {
        BackgroundColor #e1f5fe
        LineColor #01579b
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .service {
        BackgroundColor #e8f5e9
        LineColor #2e7d32
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .db {
        BackgroundColor #fff9c4
        LineColor #fbc02d
        LineThickness 2
    }
</style>

' --- Client Layer ---
package "Browser / Client Layer" as Browser_Layer {
    rectangle "Frontend Interface\n(Bootstrap-Vue)" as FE_Interface <<client>>
}

' --- Server Layer ---
package "Server Layer\n(Spring Boot)" as Server_Layer {
    
    package "Core Modules" as Core_Modules {
        ' Layout is TB within the package
        rectangle "Account Management Module" as AccountMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Q&A Community Module" as QAMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Blog Sharing Module" as BlogMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Model Management Module" as ModelMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Dataset Management Module" as DatasetMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Following Management Module" as FollowMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Admin Module" as AdminMod <<service>>
    }
}

' --- Database Layer ---
package "Database Layer\n(MySQL)" as Database_Layer {
    database "User Data" as UserDB <<db>>
    database "Q&A Community Data" as QADB <<db>>
    database "Blog Data" as BlogDB <<db>>
    database "Model Data" as ModelDB <<db>>
    database "Dataset Data" as DatasetDB <<db>>
    database "Following Data" as FollowDB <<db>>
}

' --- Relationships ---
FE_Interface --> Core_Modules
Core_Modules --> Database_Layer

@enduml

samples.gt_source:
@startuml
' --- Configuration & Styles ---
skinparam topboxSelection true
skinparam Direction top to bottom
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false

' Define Styles based on original ClassDefs
<style>
    .ui {
        BackgroundColor #ccff90
        LineColor #33691e
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .display {
        BackgroundColor #fff3e0
        LineColor #e65100
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .business {
        BackgroundColor #b2dfdb
        LineColor #004d40
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .dataLogic {
        BackgroundColor #e1bee7
        LineColor #4a148c
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .storage {
        BackgroundColor #bbdefb
        LineColor #0d47a1
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .env {
        BackgroundColor #cfd8dc
        LineColor #37474f
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .crosscut {
        BackgroundColor #ffcdd2
        LineColor #b71c1c
        LineThickness 2
    }
</style>

' --- Layer 1: UI Layer ---
package "Mini-program UI" as UI_Layer <<ui>> {
    rectangle "Wxml" as WXML <<ui>>
    rectangle "Wxss" as WXSS <<ui>>
    rectangle "JavaScript" as JS <<ui>>
}

' --- Layer 2: Display Layer ---
package "Display Layer" as Display_Layer <<display>> {
    rectangle "Template Engine Rendering\n(pages)" as Render <<display>>
    rectangle "wx.request Interaction" as Interact <<display>>
    rectangle "POST Request" as ReqPost <<display>>
    rectangle "GET Request" as ReqGet <<display>>
}

' --- Layer 3: Business Layer ---
package "Business Layer" as Business_Layer <<business>> {
    package "Core Services" as Main_Services {
        rectangle "Help Goods Service" as Svc_Goods <<business>>
        rectangle "Order Mgmt Service" as Svc_Order <<business>>
        rectangle "Personal Center Service" as Svc_User <<business>>
        rectangle "Admin Mgmt Service" as Svc_Admin <<business>>
    }

    package "Functional Modules" as Modules {
        rectangle "Hot Items Module" as Mod_Hot <<business>>
        rectangle "Create Order Module" as Mod_CreateOrder <<business>>
        rectangle "Favorites Module" as Mod_Fav <<business>>
        rectangle "Modify User Info Module" as Mod_UserInfo <<business>>
        rectangle "Goods Category Module" as Mod_Cat <<business>>
        rectangle "Order Record Module" as Mod_OrderRec <<business>>
        rectangle "Platform Mgmt Module" as Mod_Platform <<business>>
        rectangle "......" as Mod_More <<business>>
    }
}

' --- Cross-Cutting Concerns ---
rectangle "Log Recording" as Logs <<crosscut>>
rectangle "Permission Control" as Auth <<crosscut>>

' --- Layer 4: Data Layer ---
package "Data Layer" as Data_Logic_Layer <<dataLogic>> {
    rectangle "Stored Procedures" as StoredProc <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Custom Functions" as CustomFunc <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Data Cache" as DataCache <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Data Backup" as Backup <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Data Recovery" as Recovery <<dataLogic>>
    rectangle "Read/Write Database" as RW_DB <<dataLogic>>
}

' --- Layer 5: Database ---
package "Database" as Database_Storage <<storage>> {
    database "MySQL" as MySQL_Main <<storage>>
}

' --- Layer 6: Runtime Environment ---
package "Runtime Environment" as Runtime_Env <<env>> {
    node "Tencent Cloud\nLightweight App Server" as Server <<env>>
    node "MySQL 106.55.224.139\n2 Cores 4GB" as ServerSpec <<env>>
}

' --- Relationships ---
UI_Layer --> Display_Layer
Display_Layer --> Business_Layer
Interact -- ReqPost
Interact -- ReqGet

Business_Layer --> Data_Logic_Layer
Data_Logic_Layer --> Database_Storage
Runtime_Env --> Database_Storage

' Cross Cutting Links (Dotted)
Business_Layer .. Logs
Business_Layer .. Auth
Data_Logic_Layer .. Logs
Data_Logic_Layer .. Auth
Display_Layer .. Logs

@enduml

title ParaStation/psmpi — Layered Architecture Diagram

legend right
  --> : required / structural dependency
  ..> : optional / build-time conditional dependency
endlegend

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' EXTERNAL SYSTEMS
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
cloud "External Sources & OS Infrastructure" as ExtLayer {

    component "hwloc Distribution Tarball\n(FR-001, FR-002)" as HwlocTarball

    package "Autotools Toolchain" as AutotoolsPkg {
        component "autogen.sh" as Autogen
        component "configure" as Configure
        component "make / make check" as Make
    }

    package "Linux Verbs Infrastructure" as VerbsInfra {
        component "libibverbs\n(>= 1.1.8)" as Libibverbs
        component "librdmacm\n(>= 1.0.16)" as Librdmacm
        component "Matching Verbs Headers\n(build-time)" as VerbsHeaders
    }

    component "OFI/libfabric\n(system-level instance)" as OFISystem
    component "UCX\n(alternative CH4 backend)" as UCX
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' LAYER 1 — BUILD & VERIFICATION
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
package "Build & Verification Layer" as BuildLayer {

    component "EmbeddedHwlocTestRunner\n./run-embedded-tests.sh <tarball>\n(configure → make → make check\n→ clean → distclean)\n[exits 0 on success, nonzero on failure]\n(FR-001, C-002)" as EmbeddedHwlocTestRunner

    component "ManualHwlocBuildWorkflow\nhwloc-tree/ rename path\n(autogen.sh → configure → make)\n(FR-002, C-003)" as ManualHwlocBuildWorkflow

    component "BuildEnvironmentVariables\n(CFLAGS, LDFLAGS,\nLD_LIBRARY_PATH)\n(NFR-003)" as BuildEnvVars
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' LAYER 2 — COMMUNICATION SUBSYSTEM
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
package "Communication Subsystem Layer" as CommLayer {

    component "CommunicationSubsystemManager\n(init, finalize, progress,\nblocking-progress,\nconnection establishment)\n(FR-003)" as CommSubsystemMgr

    component "OneToManyTransferHandler\n(scatter-style\none-to-many transfer)\n(FR-004)" as OneToManyHandler
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' LAYER 3 — SEND STATE-MACHINE
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
package "Send State-Machine Layer" as SendSMLayer {

    component "SendStateMachine\n(flow-control gate, direct-access\nvs. packet-buffer mode,\nrndv-rts / rndv-cts / rndv-data\ncontrol-field population)\n(FR-005, FR-006, FR-007, FR-008)" as SendSM

    component "FlowControlState\n(flow-restricted /\nflow-enabled condition)\n(FR-005, DR-005)" as FlowControlState

    component "PacketBuffer\n(envelope + rendezvous\ncontrol terms: LIBA(OD),\ntwo LIBAs, one LIBA)\n(DR-001, DR-004)" as PacketBuffer

    component "NetworkSendDescriptor\n(posted-send descriptor;\nassociated with PinnedUserData\nor PacketBuffer at post time)\n(DR-002, DR-003)" as NetworkSendDescriptor

    component "DataDescriptor\n(payload descriptor;\nsource of data for packing\nor direct-access binding)\n(DR-003, FR-006, FR-007)" as DataDescriptor

    component "PinnedUserData\n(user buffer pinned\nfor zero-copy DMA)\n(DR-002, DR-003, FR-006)" as PinnedUserData

    component "NetworkDevice\n(hardware send endpoint;\nreceives posted send\ndescriptors from send path)\n(DR-002, Section 3)" as NetworkDevice
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' LAYER 4 — TRANSPORT INTEGRATION
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
package "Transport Integration Layer" as TransportLayer {

    component "CH4NetworkModuleIntegration\n(CH4 network-module binding;\nselects OFI/libfabric or UCX)\n(Section 2)" as CH4NetMod

    component "EmbeddedLibfabricModule\n(embedded Libfabric CH4 backend)" as EmbeddedLibfabric

    component "VerbsProvider\n(optional sub-component;\nenabled at build time)\n(NFR-001, NFR-002)" as VerbsProvider
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' DEPENDENCIES — Build & Verification Layer
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
HwlocTarball     --> EmbeddedHwlocTestRunner : consumed by (FR-001)
HwlocTarball     --> ManualHwlocBuildWorkflow : consumed by (FR-002)

AutotoolsPkg     --> EmbeddedHwlocTestRunner  : build toolchain
AutotoolsPkg     --> ManualHwlocBuildWorkflow : build toolchain

ManualHwlocBuildWorkflow --> BuildEnvVars : requires for non-default\npaths (NFR-003, C-003)

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' DEPENDENCIES — Build Env → Verbs (D-001 fix)
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
BuildEnvVars --> VerbsProvider : required for verbs\nbuild paths\n(NFR-003, C-004, C-005)

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' DEPENDENCIES — Send State-Machine Layer (internal)
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
SendSM --> FlowControlState      : consults before\nposting send\n(FR-005, DR-005)
SendSM --> DataDescriptor        : reads payload source\nfor packing or\ndirect-access binding\n(FR-006, FR-007, DR-003)
SendSM --> NetworkSendDescriptor : prepares per send;\nassociates with\nPinnedUserData or PacketBuffer\n(FR-006, DR-002)
SendSM --> PacketBuffer          : writes control fields;\npacks payload\n(FR-007, FR-008, DR-001)

DataDescriptor   --> PinnedUserData      : references pinned\nuser buffer\n(DR-002, DR-003)
PinnedUserData   --> NetworkSendDescriptor : bound into descriptor\nfor zero-copy DMA\n(DR-002, FR-006)
PacketBuffer     ..> NetworkSendDescriptor : associated at\nsend-post time\n(DR-001, DR-002)

NetworkSendDescriptor --> NetworkDevice  : posted send descriptor\nsubmitted to hardware\n(DR-002, Section 3)

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' DEPENDENCIES — Transport Integration Layer
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
CommSubsystemMgr --> CH4NetMod          : uses transport binding\n(FR-003)
CH4NetMod        --> EmbeddedLibfabric  : selects OFI/embedded\nbackend
CH4NetMod        --> UCX                : selects UCX backend\n(alternative)
CH4NetMod        --> OFISystem          : selects system OFI\nbackend (Section 2)
EmbeddedLibfabric --> OFISystem         : may delegate to\nsystem OFI instance
EmbeddedLibfabric ..> VerbsProvider    : contains (optional,\nbuild-time conditional)\n(NFR-001, NFR-002)

' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
' DEPENDENCIES — Verbs Provider → External OS
' ─────────────────────────────────────────────
VerbsProvider --> Libibverbs   : required >= 1.1.8\n(NFR-001, C-004, C-005)
VerbsProvider --> Librdmacm    : required >= 1.0.16\n(NFR-001, C-004, C-005)
VerbsProvider --> VerbsHeaders : compile-time header\nmatch required (NFR-002)

@enduml
samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
top to bottom direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #34495E
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "Applications\nHPC, AI, ML, Big Data" as Applications

package "Programming Models and Frameworks" as Frameworks {
    rectangle "MPI\nMPICH, Open-MPI" as MpiApps
    rectangle "PGAS / SHMEM\nOpenSHMEM, UPC, CAF, X10, Chapel" as PgasApps
    rectangle "Task Runtimes\nParsec, OCR, Legion" as TaskApps
    rectangle "AI / ML Frameworks\nTensorFlow, Burst Buffer" as MlApps
}

package "UCX" as Ucx {
    package "UCP Protocols\nHigh Level API" as Ucp {
        rectangle "Transport Selection" as TransportSelection
        rectangle "Cross-Transport Multi-Rail" as MultiRail
        rectangle "Fragmentation" as Fragmentation
        rectangle "Software Protocols" as SoftwareProtocols
        rectangle "Message Passing API\nTag Matching, Rendezvous" as MessagePassingDomain
        rectangle "PGAS API\nRMAs, Atomics" as PgasDomain
        rectangle "Task Based API\nActive Messages" as TaskDomain
        rectangle "I/O API\nStream" as IoDomain
    }

    package "UCT Hardware Transports\nLow Level API" as Uct {
        rectangle "RDMA Verbs Transport\nInfiniBand, ROCE, OPA" as RdmaTransport
        rectangle "RC" as Rc
        rectangle "UD" as Ud
        rectangle "DCT" as Dct
        rectangle "Gemini / Aries Transport\nGNI" as GniTransport
        rectangle "Intra-node Host Memory\nSYSV, POSIX, KNEM, CMA, XPMEM" as HostMemoryTransport
        rectangle "GPU Transport\nNVIDIA CUDA, AMD ROCm" as GpuTransport
        rectangle "Ethernet Transport\nTCP/IP Sockets" as EthernetTransport
    }

    package "UCS Services\nCommon Utilities" as Ucs {
        rectangle "Utilities" as Utilities
        rectangle "Data Structures" as DataStructures
        rectangle "Memory Management" as MemoryManagement
    }

    package "UCM Memory Services" as Ucm {
        rectangle "Memory Hooks" as MemoryHooks
    }
}

package "Drivers and Kernels" as DriverLayer {
    rectangle "RDMA Verbs Driver" as RdmaDriver
    rectangle "Cray Driver" as CrayDriver
    rectangle "OS Kernel\nHost Memory" as HostKernel
    rectangle "ROCm / CUDA" as GpuRuntime
    rectangle "OS Kernel\nEthernet" as EthernetKernel
    rectangle "OS Kernel\nMemory Hooks" as MemoryKernel
}

rectangle "Hardware" as Hardware

Applications --> MpiApps
Applications --> PgasApps
Applications --> TaskApps
Applications --> MlApps

MpiApps --> MessagePassingDomain
PgasApps --> PgasDomain
TaskApps --> TaskDomain
MlApps --> IoDomain

MessagePassingDomain --> TransportSelection
PgasDomain --> TransportSelection
TaskDomain --> TransportSelection
IoDomain --> TransportSelection
TransportSelection --> MultiRail
MultiRail --> Fragmentation
Fragmentation --> SoftwareProtocols

SoftwareProtocols --> RdmaTransport
SoftwareProtocols --> GniTransport
SoftwareProtocols --> HostMemoryTransport
SoftwareProtocols --> GpuTransport
SoftwareProtocols --> EthernetTransport

RdmaTransport --> Rc
RdmaTransport --> Ud
RdmaTransport --> Dct

RdmaTransport --> RdmaDriver
GniTransport --> CrayDriver
HostMemoryTransport --> HostKernel
GpuTransport --> GpuRuntime
EthernetTransport --> EthernetKernel
MemoryHooks --> MemoryKernel

Ucp --> Ucs : common utilities
Uct --> Ucs : utility services
Uct --> Ucm : memory hooks

RdmaDriver --> Hardware
CrayDriver --> Hardware
HostKernel --> Hardware
GpuRuntime --> Hardware
EthernetKernel --> Hardware
MemoryKernel --> Hardware
@enduml

@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
top to bottom direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #6A00FF
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #888888
    BorderThickness 1
}

    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #AAAAAA
    BorderThickness 1

rectangle "Data Engineer" as DataEngineer

package "Docker Compose Environment" as ComposeEnvironment {
    rectangle "Postgres" as Postgres
    rectangle "Airflow\n(Spark Driver)" as Airflow
    rectangle "Jupyter Notebook\n(Spark)" as JupyterNotebook
    rectangle "Cluster Manager\n(Spark Standalone)" as ClusterManager

    package "Spark Workers" as SparkWorkers {
        rectangle "Worker 1" as Worker1
        rectangle "Worker 2" as Worker2
        rectangle "Worker 3" as Worker3
        rectangle "Worker N" as WorkerN
    }
}

DataEngineer --> Postgres : access
DataEngineer --> Airflow : schedule and observe DAGs
DataEngineer --> JupyterNotebook : interactive notebooks
Airflow <--> Postgres : metadata
Airflow --> ClusterManager : Spark job submission
ClusterManager --> Worker1 : dispatch workloads
ClusterManager --> Worker2 : dispatch workloads
ClusterManager --> Worker3 : dispatch workloads
ClusterManager --> WorkerN : dispatch workloads
@enduml
