samples.title: Indoor_Fitness_App / mini-swe-agent/claude-sonnet-4-6
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam linetype ortho

title Indoor Fitness App - Layered Architecture

package "Mobile Client" {
    [UI Layer] as UI
    [Camera and Video] as Camera
    [Pose Estimation Engine] as PoseEngine
    [Screen Casting Client] as CastClient
    [Local Storage] as LocalStorage
}

package "Backend Server" {
    [Auth Service] as AuthService
    [Training Plan Service] as PlanService
    [Posture Evaluation Service] as EvalService
    [Report and Poster Generator] as ReportService
    [User Management Service] as UserMgmt
    [Screen Cast Server] as CastServer
}

package "Data Layer" {
    database "User DB" as UserDB
    database "Training Store" as TrainingDB
    database "Movement Library" as MovementDB
    database "Media Store" as MediaDB
}

package "External Systems" {
    [Android Target Device] as AndroidDevice
    [Mobile Camera Hardware] as CameraHW
}

UI --> Camera
Camera --> CameraHW
Camera --> PoseEngine : frames
PoseEngine --> UI : skeleton and score
UI --> CastClient
CastClient --> CastServer : LAN stream

UI --> AuthService : login and register
UI --> PlanService : request plan
UI --> EvalService : posture data
UI --> ReportService : poster or report
UI --> UserMgmt : profile update
UI --> TrainingDB : history read

PoseEngine --> EvalService : keypoints

AuthService --> UserDB
PlanService --> MovementDB
PlanService --> TrainingDB
EvalService --> MovementDB
EvalService --> TrainingDB
ReportService --> TrainingDB
ReportService --> MediaDB
UserMgmt --> UserDB

CastServer --> AndroidDevice : video stream
CastServer --> CastClient : control commands

@enduml
samples.gt_source:
@startuml
' --- Configuration & Styles ---
skinparam ranksep 50
skinparam nodesep 40
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
left to right direction

' Styling for different layers
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor<<client>> #e1f5fe
    BorderColor<<client>> #01579b
    
    BackgroundColor<<native>> #bbdefb
    BorderColor<<native>> #0d47a1
    
    BackgroundColor<<react>> #c8e6c9
    BorderColor<<react>> #1b5e20
    
    BackgroundColor<<bridge>> #00acc1
    BorderColor<<bridge>> #006064
    FontColor<<bridge>> white
    
    BackgroundColor<<infra>> #f5f5f5
    BorderColor<<infra>> #212121
    BorderStyle<<infra>> dashed
}

' --- Diagram Definitions ---

' Layer 1: Client Layer
package "Client / Platforms" as ClientLayer <<client>> {
    rectangle "Mobile Device" as Mobile <<client>>
    rectangle "Desktop PC" as Desktop <<client>>
    rectangle "Web Browser" as Web <<client>>
    rectangle "Android OS" as Android <<client>>
    rectangle "iOS / Apple" as iOS <<client>>
    rectangle "Smart Watch / Wearables" as IoT <<client>>
}

' Layer 2: Native Module Layer
package "Native Modules" as NativeLayer <<native>> {
    rectangle "Camera Call" as Camera <<native>>
    rectangle "Model Loading / TensorFlow" as ModelLoad <<native>>
    rectangle "Linear Algebra Library" as LinearAlgebra <<native>>
    rectangle "Voice Library Call" as Voice <<native>>
    rectangle "GPU Computing" as GPU <<native>>
    rectangle "Screen Recording" as ScreenRec <<native>>
    rectangle "Socket Communication" as Socket <<native>>
    rectangle "Data Processing" as DataProc <<native>>
    rectangle "Permission Requests" as Permissions <<native>>
}

' Layer 3: Cross-Platform Framework Layer
package "React Native Framework" as RNLayer <<react>> {
    rectangle "Native Module Bridge" as NativeBridge <<bridge>>
    rectangle "Data Caching" as DataCaching <<react>>
    rectangle "Server Access" as ServerAccess <<react>>
    rectangle "Redux State Management" as Redux <<react>>
    rectangle "Interface Packaging" as APIPkg <<react>>
    rectangle "Data Interception" as DataIntercept <<react>>
    rectangle "End-to-End Communication" as P2P <<react>>
}

' Sidebar: Infrastructure
package "Infrastructure & Support" as InfraLayer <<infra>> {
    rectangle "Data Exchange" as DataExchange <<infra>>
    rectangle "Performance Optimization" as Performance <<infra>>
    rectangle "Stability Construction" as Stability <<infra>>
}

' --- Relationships ---

ClientLayer --> NativeLayer
NativeLayer <--> NativeBridge

' Directing bridge to RN components
NativeBridge --- DataCaching
NativeBridge --- ServerAccess
NativeBridge --- Redux
NativeBridge --- APIPkg
NativeBridge --- DataIntercept
NativeBridge --- P2P

' Infrastructure interaction (dashed as per original)
NativeLayer ..> InfraLayer
RNLayer ..> InfraLayer

@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The architecture covers most functional requirements and data requirements well. All major functional areas (pose evaluation, plan recommendation, sharing, casting, history, security) are represented by components and interactions, and all data stores are present. However, it lacks explicit architectural elements or patterns to address critical non-functional requirements such as SQL injection prevention (NFR-013) and data leakage protection (NFR-012). While modularity (NFR-014) is implicitly shown, its explicit 'interfaces for data exchange' aspect is not a distinct architectural element.

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram accurately represents many aspects of the requirements, such as the mobile client, backend services for plans and reports, and data storage. However, there are two significant deviations from the explicit SRS wording: 1. The SRS states that 'Casting video stream... is transmitted between Android devices' and 'Android server-to-client commands' implies the source device acts as a server. The diagram introduces a 'Screen Cast Server' in the backend, mediating this communication, which is an unfaithful interpretation. 2. FR-004 and the 'Posture evaluation processing interface' explicitly state that 'The terminal device feeds back skeleton extraction information in real time and compares it with standard skeleton movements for scoring.' The diagram shows 'Pose Estimation Engine' (client) sending 'keypoints' to a 'Posture Evaluation Service' (backend server), implying server-side comparison or significant processing, which contradicts the SRS's emphasis on client-side real-time evaluation.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The architecture demonstrates good separation of concerns and modularity, which are sound architectural principles. Dedicated services for authentication, training plans, and reports are rational. However, the architectural decisions regarding screen casting and posture evaluation introduce potential issues: 1. Routing casting video through a backend 'Screen Cast Server' for LAN communication (as depicted) could introduce unnecessary latency and complexity, potentially jeopardizing NFR-010 (Casting Video Latency < 100ms) and NFR-011 (Casting Command Latency < 50ms), which are critical real-time requirements for local device-to-device communication. 2. Offloading real-time posture comparison (as implied by PoseEngine sending keypoints to EvalService on the server) could also introduce latency, conflicting with the 'real-time' aspect emphasized in FR-004 and NFR-004. 3. The absence of explicit architectural patterns or components for critical security NFRs like data leakage protection (NFR-012) and SQL injection prevention (NFR-013) indicates a gap in addressing these significant quality attributes.

traceability.reasoning: Functional requirements are generally traceable to specific components and their interactions (e.g., 'PlanService' to FR-007, FR-008; 'ReportService' to FR-009, FR-010). Data requirements are also well-mapped to the data layer. However, traceability for non-functional requirements, especially security (NFR-012, NFR-013) and the specific 'inter-module data exchange interface' (NFR-014), is weak as these are not explicitly represented or addressed by distinct architectural elements.

readability.reasoning: The diagram is highly readable, utilizing clear component names, logical grouping into packages (Mobile Client, Backend Server, Data Layer, External Systems), and orthogonal connectors. The overall layout is clean and easy to follow, making it simple to understand the high-level structure and interactions.

samples.title: s000038_2c7731fc / metagpt-custom/claude-sonnet-4-6
samples.pred_source:
@startuml IceFireDB Architecture

skinparam backgroundColor #FAFAFA
skinparam packageBackgroundColor #EEF2FF
skinparam componentBackgroundColor #DBEAFE
skinparam nodeBackgroundColor #FEF9C3
skinparam databaseBackgroundColor #DCFCE7
skinparam cloudBackgroundColor #FCE7F3
skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam arrowColor #374151
skinparam packageBorderColor #6366F1
skinparam componentBorderColor #3B82F6
skinparam nodeBorderColor #CA8A04
skinparam databaseBorderColor #16A34A

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' EXTERNAL SYSTEMS
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

cloud "External Clients" as ExtClients {
    component [Redis-based\nApplication Client] as AppClient
}

cloud "libp2p / IPFS Peer Network" as LibP2PCloud {
    component [DHT / libp2p\nPeer Infrastructure\n(peer discovery, routing\n& NAT traversal)] as LibP2PPeer
}

cloud "MySQL/SQLite Protocol\nData Source" as MySQLSource {
    component [Packetized MySQL-Protocol\nResult Data] as PacketizedData
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' CLIENT INTERFACE LAYER
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

package "Client Interface Layer" as ClientLayer {
    () "Redis Protocol API" as RedisAPI
    () "Redis Pub/Sub API" as PubSubAPI
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' DECENTRALIZED MIDDLEWARE LAYER
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

package "Decentralized Middleware Layer" as MiddlewareLayer {
    component [RedisProxyAgent\n\nSupported command categories:\nString, Set, List, Hash,\nSorted Set, Stream,\nBasic Control Commands] as RedisProxyAgent
    component [AgentSyncNetwork] as AgentSyncNetwork
    component [PubSubNode] as PubSubNode
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' P2P NETWORK LAYER
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

package "P2P Network Layer" as P2PLayer {
    component [PeerDiscoveryRouter\n(Kademlia DHT / libp2p)] as PeerDiscoveryRouter
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' STORAGE BACKEND LAYER
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

package "Storage Backend Layer" as StorageLayer {
    component [RedisStandaloneAdapter] as RedisStandaloneAdapter
    component [RedisClusterAdapter] as RedisClusterAdapter
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' INTERNAL PROTOCOL HANDLING LAYER
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

package "Internal Protocol Handling Layer" as InternalLayer {
    component [SQLiteResultParser\n(MySQL-Protocol Parser)] as SQLiteResultParser
    note right of SQLiteResultParser
        Parses: field metadata,
        field-name mappings,
        result rows, warning
        counts, status values
    end note
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' EXTERNAL STORAGE NODES
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

node "Redis Standalone Instance" as RedisStandalone {
    database "Redis\nSingle-Node Store" as RedisDB
}

node "Redis Cluster" as RedisClusterNode {
    database "Redis Cluster\nStore (multi-node)" as RedisClusterDB
}

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' CLIENT → INTERFACE CONNECTIONS
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

AppClient --> RedisAPI : Redis commands\n(String/Set/List/Hash/\nSortedSet/Stream/Control)
AppClient --> PubSubAPI : Pub/Sub publish\n& subscribe operations

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' INTERFACE → MIDDLEWARE CONNECTIONS
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

RedisAPI --> RedisProxyAgent : validated Redis commands
PubSubAPI --> PubSubNode : Pub/Sub commands

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' MIDDLEWARE INTERNAL CONNECTIONS
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

RedisProxyAgent --> AgentSyncNetwork : propagate instructions\nto peer agents
AgentSyncNetwork --> RedisProxyAgent : synchronized instructions\nfrom peer agents
AgentSyncNetwork --> PeerDiscoveryRouter : decentralized agent\nnetwork transport

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' MIDDLEWARE → STORAGE CONNECTIONS
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

RedisProxyAgent --> RedisStandaloneAdapter : forwarded writes\n(standalone mode)
RedisProxyAgent --> RedisClusterAdapter : forwarded writes\n(cluster mode)

AgentSyncNetwork --> RedisStandaloneAdapter : synchronized writes\n(standalone mode)
AgentSyncNetwork --> RedisClusterAdapter : synchronized writes\n(cluster mode)

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' PUB/SUB NODE CONNECTIONS
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

PubSubNode --> PeerDiscoveryRouter : peer discovery\n& message routing
PubSubNode --> AppClient : delivered messages\nto subscribers
PubSubNode --> PubSubNode : inter-node message\ndissemination via P2P layer

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' P2P LAYER → EXTERNAL NETWORK
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

PeerDiscoveryRouter --> LibP2PPeer : DHT peer discovery\n& routing (libp2p/IPFS)

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' STORAGE ADAPTERS → EXTERNAL STORES
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

RedisStandaloneAdapter --> RedisDB : read / write
RedisClusterAdapter --> RedisClusterDB : cluster-aware\nread / write / failover

' ─────────────────────────────────────────
' INTERNAL LAYER CONNECTIONS
' ─────────────────────────────────────────

PacketizedData --> SQLiteResultParser : consumes packetized\nMySQL-protocol results

@enduml
samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #333333
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #444444
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "Web2 application stack" as Web2Stack {
    rectangle "Applications" as Web2Applications
    rectangle "Platform" as Web2Platform
    rectangle "Processing\nBusiness Logic" as Web2Processing
    rectangle "File System\nLarge Files and Media" as Web2FileSystem
    database "BlockChain Database\nTransactions, Contracts,\nCertificates and Receipts" as Web2BlockchainDatabase
    rectangle "IceFireDB" as Web2IceFireDB
}

package "Web3 application stack" as Web3Stack {
    rectangle "Applications" as Web3Applications
    rectangle "Platform" as Web3Platform
    rectangle "Decentralized Processing\nBusiness Logic" as Web3Processing
    rectangle "Decentralized File System\nLarge Files and Media" as Web3FileSystem
    database "BlockChain Database\nTransactions, Contracts,\nCertificates and Receipts" as Web3BlockchainDatabase
    rectangle "IceFireDB" as Web3IceFireDB
}

Web2Applications --> Web2Platform
Web2Platform --> Web2Processing
Web2Processing --> Web2FileSystem
Web2Processing --> Web2BlockchainDatabase
Web2BlockchainDatabase --> Web2IceFireDB

Web3Applications --> Web3Platform
Web3Platform --> Web3Processing
Web3Processing --> Web3FileSystem
Web3Processing --> Web3BlockchainDatabase
Web3BlockchainDatabase --> Web3IceFireDB

Web2IceFireDB ..> Web3IceFireDB : decentralized database support
@enduml

samples.title: AwesomeMusic / metagpt-custom/claude-sonnet-4-6
samples.pred_source:
@startuml AwesomeMusic_Architecture_Refined

skinparam backgroundColor #FAFAFA
skinparam packageBackgroundColor #EEF2FF
skinparam componentBackgroundColor #FFFFFF
skinparam nodeBackgroundColor #FFF8E1
skinparam databaseBackgroundColor #E8F5E9
skinparam cloudBackgroundColor #E3F2FD
skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam defaultFontSize 12
skinparam arrowColor #455A64
skinparam packageBorderColor #7986CB
skinparam componentBorderColor #90A4AE
skinparam nodeBorderColor #FFB300

' =============================================
' PRESENTATION LAYER
' =============================================
package "Presentation Layer" as PL #EEF2FF {
    component [WebFrontend\n(HTML/CSS/JS/Bootstrap)] as WebFrontend
}

' =============================================
' GATEWAY LAYER
' =============================================
package "Gateway Layer" as GL #FCE4EC {
    node "Nginx 1.19.8\n(Reverse Proxy / Load Balancer)" as NginxGateway {
        component [HTTPS Termination\n& Traffic Distribution] as NginxCore
    }
}

' =============================================
' APPLICATION LAYER
' =============================================
package "Application Layer" as AL #E8F5E9 {
    node "Backend API Server Cluster\n(Golang / Gin)" as BackendCluster {
        component [BackendAPIServer\n(REST Interfaces)] as BackendAPIServer
        component [UserAccountManager\n(Registration / Login / Auth)] as UserAccountManager
        component [ScoreContentManager\n(Score CRUD / Search / Download)] as ScoreContentManager
        component [RecommendationManager\n(Assemble Recommendation Lists)] as RecommendationManager
        component [HotChartUpdater\n(Attention & Popularity Charts)] as HotChartUpdater
    }
}

' =============================================
' SCHEDULING LAYER
' =============================================
package "Scheduling Layer" as SL #FFF3E0 {
    node "Scheduling Module\n(Task Coordinator)" as SchedulingModule {
        component [TaskSerializer\n(Serialize Task Metadata)] as TaskSerializer
        component [TaskPoller\n(Poll Status / Retry / Log Errors)] as TaskPoller
        component [TimeTrigger\n(Daily 02:00 Cron)] as TimeTrigger
        component [UserTrigger\n(On-Demand Submission)] as UserTrigger
    }
}

' =============================================
' ALGORITHM LAYER
' =============================================
package "Algorithm Layer" as ALG #F3E5F5 {
    node "Algorithm Service\n(ML Orchestrator)" as AlgorithmService {
        component [ScoreGenerationEngine\n(GPT-2 / MIDI↔ABC)] as ScoreGenerationEngine
        component [ModelTrainingEngine\n(GPT-2 Fine-Tuning / Daily)] as ModelTrainingEngine
        component [ScoreClassificationEngine\n(LSTM Classification / Daily)] as ScoreClassificationEngine
        component [RecommendationEngine\n(Cosine Similarity / LSTM)] as RecommendationEngine
    }
}

' =============================================
' INFRASTRUCTURE / DATA LAYER
' =============================================
package "Infrastructure / Data Layer" as IDL #ECEFF1 {
    database "PersistentDataStore\n(3-Replica)\nUsers | Scores | Favorites |\nFollows | Labels | Representations |\nTask Metadata | Hot Charts | Logs" as PersistentDataStore

    node "Apache Hadoop 3.2.2\n(Distributed File Storage)" as HadoopStorage {
        component [MIDI Files\n(≤4 min)] as HadoopMidi
        component [ABC Score Files] as HadoopABC
        component [GPT-2 Model Parameters] as HadoopModels
        component [LSTM Score Representations\n(Binary Vectors)] as HadoopLSTM
    }

    node "RabbitMQ 3.8.14\n(Message Broker)" as RabbitMQ {
        component [Task Queue\n(Training / Classification /\nRecommendation / Generation)] as TaskQueue
    }

    node "etcd 3.4.15\n(Distributed Coordination)" as etcd {
        component [Distributed Lock\n& Coordination Store] as etcdCore
    }
}

' =============================================
' EXTERNAL SYSTEMS
' =============================================
cloud "External Clients" as ExtClients #E3F2FD {
    component [MIDI-Capable Player\n(External Client-Side)] as ExtMidi
}

' =============================================
' ARROWS — PRESENTATION → GATEWAY
' =============================================
WebFrontend -down-> NginxGateway : HTTPS Requests

' =============================================
' ARROWS — GATEWAY → APPLICATION
' =============================================
NginxGateway -down-> BackendAPIServer : Proxy & Load Balance\n(≥2000 QPS)

' =============================================
' ARROWS — APPLICATION RESPONSE TO FRONTEND
' =============================================
BackendAPIServer -up-> NginxGateway : Response / File Delivery\n(MIDI Download ≤3000ms, NFR-008)

' =============================================
' ARROWS — APPLICATION INTERNAL COMPOSITION
' =============================================
BackendAPIServer -down-> UserAccountManager : delegates auth
BackendAPIServer -down-> ScoreContentManager : delegates score ops
BackendAPIServer -down-> RecommendationManager : delegates recommendations
BackendAPIServer -down-> HotChartUpdater : delegates chart updates

' =============================================
' ARROWS — APPLICATION → SCHEDULING
' =============================================
BackendAPIServer -down-> SchedulingModule : Trigger Generation Task\n(User-Submitted / FR-017)

' =============================================
' ARROWS — APPLICATION → INFRASTRUCTURE
' =============================================
BackendAPIServer -down-> PersistentDataStore : Read / Write\n(Users, Scores, Tasks, Charts)
BackendAPIServer -right-> HadoopStorage : Store / Retrieve\n(MIDI Files, ABC Score Files)

' =============================================
' ARROWS — SCHEDULING INTERNAL
' =============================================
TimeTrigger -right-> TaskSerializer : fire at 02:00 daily\n(Training / Classification /\nRecommendation — FR-016)
UserTrigger -right-> TaskSerializer : fire on submission\n(Generation — FR-017)

' =============================================
' ARROWS — SCHEDULING → INFRASTRUCTURE
' =============================================
SchedulingModule -down-> RabbitMQ : Push Task Metadata\nto Queue
SchedulingModule -right-> etcd : Distributed Coordination\n(Avoid Duplicate Firing)
SchedulingModule -down-> PersistentDataStore : Write Task Status\n& Error Logs\n(FR-016 / FR-017 / DR-015)

' =============================================
' ARROWS — SCHEDULING → ALGORITHM (offline daily batch — FR-016)
' =============================================
SchedulingModule -down-> AlgorithmService : Trigger Offline Daily Batch\n(Training / Classification /\nRecommendation — FR-016)

' =============================================
' ARROWS — TASK POLLER
' =============================================
TaskPoller -down-> RabbitMQ : Poll Status / Retry\non Failure

' =============================================
' ARROWS — RABBITMQ → ALGORITHM
' =============================================
RabbitMQ -down-> AlgorithmService : Consume Generation Tasks\n(User-Triggered / FR-017)\n& Publish Status

' =============================================
' ARROWS — ALGORITHM → INFRASTRUCTURE
' =============================================
AlgorithmService -down-> HadoopStorage : Read MIDI/ABC Sources\nWrite Model Params &\nGenerated Scores
ScoreClassificationEngine -down-> HadoopLSTM : Write LSTM Score\nRepresentations (DR-012)
RecommendationEngine -down-> HadoopLSTM : Read LSTM Score\nRepresentations (DR-012)
AlgorithmService -down-> PersistentDataStore : Read Playlists / Score Lists\nWrite Labels / Recommendation Lists /\nExceptions / Representations

' =============================================
' ARROWS — ALGORITHM → APPLICATION (Callbacks)
' =============================================
AlgorithmService -up-> BackendAPIServer : Exception Notifications\n(Conversion Failure / FR-013 / FR-014)

' =============================================
' ARROWS — ALGORITHM → SCHEDULING (FR-012 Exception Path)
' =============================================
ModelTrainingEngine -up-> SchedulingModule : Exception Notification\n(MIDI Conversion Failure / FR-012)

' =============================================
' ARROWS — PRESENTATION MISC
' =============================================
WebFrontend -right-> ExtMidi : Download MIDI File\n& Play Externally (FR-011)

' =============================================
' NOTES
' =============================================
note bottom of PersistentDataStore
  3-replica deployment required.
  Stores: task status, error logs,
  user data, scores, charts,
  labels, and representations.
end note

note bottom of SchedulingModule
  Handles both time-triggered (02:00 daily)
  and user-triggered generation workflows.
  Logs errors and retries on failure.
  Writes all task status and logs to
  PersistentDataStore (DR-015).
end note

note right of AlgorithmService
  Online: ScoreGenerationEngine (FR-017)
  Offline (daily, FR-016):
    ModelTrainingEngine
    ScoreClassificationEngine
    RecommendationEngine
  FR-012: ModelTrainingEngine notifies
  SchedulingModule on MIDI conversion failure.
end note

note right of HadoopLSTM
  DR-012: LSTM score representations
  persisted to Hadoop for use by
  RecommendationEngine.
end note

@enduml

left to right direction

  component "Browser UI\n(Front-end SPA)" as UI
}

package "Edge" {

}

package "Backend API" {

  component "Realtime Gateway\n(WebSocket)" as WSGW
}

package "User & Access" {

  component "User Service" as UserSvc
}

package "Collaboration & Projects" {
  component "Project Service" as ProjectSvc
  component "Group Service" as GroupSvc

  component "Comment Service" as CommentSvc
  component "Collaboration Service" as CollabSvc
}

package "File & Processing Pipeline" {

  component "File Parser\n(CSV/Excel/PDF)" as Parser
  component "Recognition Service\n(OCR + DL Models)" as OCRSvc
  component "LaTeX Generator" as LatexGen
  component "Rendering Service\n(LaTeX Engine)" as RenderSvc
  component "Export Service" as ExportSvc
}

package "Data Stores" {
  database "Relational DB\n(MySQL/PostgreSQL)" as DB
  folder "File / Object Storage" as FS
}

UI --> WSGW : WebSocket

RenderSvc --> FS : render artifacts

LatexGen --> RenderSvc : LaTeX code

samples.title: ModelSharePlatform / direct/claude-sonnet-4-6
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
!define RECTANGLE class

skinparam backgroundColor #FAFAFA
skinparam node {
  BackgroundColor #DDEEFF
  BorderColor #336699
  FontColor #000000
}
skinparam component {
  BackgroundColor #FFFDE7
  BorderColor #F9A825
  FontColor #000000
}
skinparam database {
  BackgroundColor #E8F5E9
  BorderColor #388E3C
  FontColor #000000
}
skinparam rectangle {
  BackgroundColor #F3E5F5
  BorderColor #7B1FA2
  FontColor #000000
}
skinparam cloud {
  BackgroundColor #E3F2FD
  BorderColor #1565C0
  FontColor #000000
}
skinparam arrow {
  Color #555555
}
skinparam defaultFontSize 12

title Deep Learning Model Sharing Platform — System Architecture

actor "General User" as GU
actor "Administrator" as ADMIN
actor "Unregistered User" as UU

cloud "Client Layer\n(Modern Browsers — IE Not Supported)" as CLIENT {
  component "Vue + TypeScript\nFrontend SPA" as VUE {
    component "Account UI\n(Register / Login / Password Recovery\nProfile / Logout)" as UI_ACCOUNT
    component "Q&A Community UI\n(Questions / Answers\nPublish / Modify / Delete)" as UI_QA
    component "Blog UI\n(Articles / Comments\nPublish / Modify / Delete)" as UI_BLOG
    component "Model UI\n(Upload / Download / Purchase\nOnline Invocation / Recommendation)" as UI_MODEL
    component "Dataset UI\n(Upload / Download\nModify / Delete)" as UI_DATASET
    component "Follow UI\n(Follow / Unfollow\nUser Profiles)" as UI_FOLLOW
    component "Admin UI\n(Account Cancellation\nIllegal Content Deletion)" as UI_ADMIN
    component "Multilingual UI\n(Chinese / English)" as UI_I18N
  }
}

node "Application Server Layer\n(Spring Boot)" as APPSERVER {

  rectangle "API Gateway / Security Filter\n(Auth, Session, Injection Prevention)" as GATEWAY

  rectangle "Account Service\n(FR-001 to FR-005)\n- Registration & Validation\n- Login & Session\n- Password Recovery (Email)\n- Profile Management\n- Logout" as SVC_ACCOUNT

  rectangle "Q&A Community Service\n(FR-006 to FR-011)\n- Publish / Modify / Delete Questions\n- Publish / Modify / Delete Answers\n- Save Prompt & Exception Feedback" as SVC_QA

  rectangle "Blog Service\n(FR-012 to FR-017)\n- Publish / Modify / Delete Articles\n- Publish / Modify / Delete Comments\n- Comment Validation & Refresh" as SVC_BLOG

  rectangle "Model Management Service\n(FR-018 to FR-024)\n- Upload & Review Entry\n- Download (Purchased)\n- Modify / Delete Uploaded Models\n- Online Invocation\n- Purchase & Payment\n- Recommendation Engine" as SVC_MODEL

  rectangle "Dataset Management Service\n(FR-025 to FR-028)\n- Upload & Naming Validation\n- Free Download\n- Delete / Modify Datasets" as SVC_DATASET

  rectangle "Follow Service\n(FR-029 to FR-030)\n- Follow / Unfollow Users\n- Follow List Management\n- Abbreviated User Profile" as SVC_FOLLOW

  rectangle "Administrator Service\n(FR-031 to FR-032)\n- Cancel User Accounts\n- Delete Illegal Content\n(Questions / Answers / Blogs /\nComments / Models / Datasets)" as SVC_ADMIN

  rectangle "Recommendation Engine\n(NFR-015, DR-009)\n- Browsing History Analysis\n- Registration Info Analysis\n- Traditional + Intelligent Algorithms\n- Model Pool Generation" as REC_ENGINE

  rectangle "Email / Notification Service\n(FR-003)\n- Verification Code Email\n- Password Recovery" as SVC_EMAIL

  rectangle "Security Module\n(NFR-001, NFR-002, NFR-003)\n- Injection Attack Prevention\n- User Authentication\n- Role-Based Access Control\n- Admin Function Restriction" as SEC_MODULE
}

node "Storage Layer" as STORAGE {
  database "MySQL Database\n(Primary Relational Store)" as MYSQL {
    component "User Account Data\n(DR-001)\nRegistration, Login State,\nEmail, Password, Role" as DB_USER
    component "Q&A Data\n(DR-002, DR-003)\nQuestions & Answers" as DB_QA
    component "Blog Data\n(DR-004, DR-005)\nArticles & Comments" as DB_BLOG
    component "Model Data\n(DR-006)\nBasic Info, Files, Review State,\nSale Flag, Price, Purchases" as DB_MODEL
    component "Dataset Data\n(DR-007)\nName, Path, File, Owner" as DB_DATASET
    component "Follow Relationship Data\n(DR-008)\nFollow Lists" as DB_FOLLOW
    component "Recommendation Data\n(DR-009)\nBrowsing History,\nRegistration Info" as DB_REC
    component "Admin Governance Data\n(DR-010)\nDeleted Content Records" as DB_ADMIN
  }

  database "File / Object Storage\n(Model Files & Dataset Files)" as FILESTORAGE
}

node "Infrastructure Layer" as INFRA {
  component "x86-64 / ARM CPU\n≥4 GB RAM / ≥30 GB Disk\n(C-002, C-003, C-004)" as HW
  component "Runtime Environment\n(Java / Node Build)" as RUNTIME
  component "Maintenance Window\n01:00–04:00\n(NFR-008)" as MAINT
  component "Fault Recovery\n< 1 Hour Recovery\n(NFR-011)" as FAULT
}

' --- Client to Gateway ---
UU --> VUE : Browse / Register
GU --> VUE : All User Functions
ADMIN --> VUE : Admin Functions

VUE --> GATEWAY : HTTPS Requests

' --- Gateway to Services ---
GATEWAY --> SVC_ACCOUNT
GATEWAY --> SVC_QA
GATEWAY --> SVC_BLOG
GATEWAY --> SVC_MODEL
GATEWAY --> SVC_DATASET
GATEWAY --> SVC_FOLLOW
GATEWAY --> SVC_ADMIN
GATEWAY --> SEC_MODULE

' --- Service Dependencies ---
SVC_MODEL --> REC_ENGINE : Trigger Recommendation
SVC_MODEL --> FILESTORAGE : Upload / Download Model Files
SVC_DATASET --> FILESTORAGE : Upload / Download Dataset Files
SVC_ACCOUNT --> SVC_EMAIL : Send Recovery Email
SVC_MODEL --> SVC_DATASET : Online Invocation (Dataset Input)

SEC_MODULE --> GATEWAY : Enforce Policies

' --- Services to DB ---
SVC_ACCOUNT --> DB_USER
SVC_QA --> DB_QA
SVC_BLOG --> DB_BLOG
SVC_MODEL --> DB_MODEL
SVC_DATASET --> DB_DATASET
SVC_FOLLOW --> DB_FOLLOW
REC_ENGINE --> DB_REC
SVC_ADMIN --> DB_ADMIN
SVC_ADMIN --> DB_USER
SVC_ADMIN --> DB_QA
SVC_ADMIN --> DB_BLOG
SVC_ADMIN --> DB_MODEL
SVC_ADMIN --> DB_DATASET

' --- Storage to Infra ---
MYSQL --> INFRA
FILESTORAGE --> INFRA
APPSERVER --> INFRA

' --- UI components to Vue ---
UI_ACCOUNT -[hidden]- UI_QA
UI_QA -[hidden]- UI_BLOG
UI_BLOG -[hidden]- UI_MODEL
UI_MODEL -[hidden]- UI_DATASET
UI_DATASET -[hidden]- UI_FOLLOW
UI_FOLLOW -[hidden]- UI_ADMIN
UI_ADMIN -[hidden]- UI_I18N

note right of SEC_MODULE
  NFR-001: Prevent malicious operations
  NFR-002: Block injection attacks
  NFR-003: Secure authentication & admin control
end note

note right of MYSQL
  NFR-004: Server/front-end data consistency
  within 3-second interval
  NFR-009: Supports >10,000 users
end note

note bottom of APPSERVER
  NFR-010: 1,000 requests/sec throughput
  NFR-007: MTTF > 1 year
  NFR-014: Extensible via OO/abstraction/modules
  C-001: Spring Boot, Vue, TypeScript, MySQL, SQL
end note

@enduml

@startuml
' --- Configuration & Styles ---
skinparam topboxSelection true
skinparam Direction top to bottom
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false

' Define Styles based on original ClassDefs
<style>
    .client {
        BackgroundColor #e1f5fe
        LineColor #01579b
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .service {
        BackgroundColor #e8f5e9
        LineColor #2e7d32
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .db {
        BackgroundColor #fff9c4
        LineColor #fbc02d
        LineThickness 2
    }
</style>

' --- Client Layer ---
package "Browser / Client Layer" as Browser_Layer {
    rectangle "Frontend Interface\n(Bootstrap-Vue)" as FE_Interface <<client>>
}

' --- Server Layer ---
package "Server Layer\n(Spring Boot)" as Server_Layer {
    
    package "Core Modules" as Core_Modules {
        ' Layout is TB within the package
        rectangle "Account Management Module" as AccountMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Q&A Community Module" as QAMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Blog Sharing Module" as BlogMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Model Management Module" as ModelMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Dataset Management Module" as DatasetMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Following Management Module" as FollowMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Admin Module" as AdminMod <<service>>
    }
}

' --- Database Layer ---
package "Database Layer\n(MySQL)" as Database_Layer {
    database "User Data" as UserDB <<db>>
    database "Q&A Community Data" as QADB <<db>>
    database "Blog Data" as BlogDB <<db>>
    database "Model Data" as ModelDB <<db>>
    database "Dataset Data" as DatasetDB <<db>>
    database "Following Data" as FollowDB <<db>>
}

' --- Relationships ---
FE_Interface --> Core_Modules
Core_Modules --> Database_Layer

@enduml

samples.title: AwesomeMusic / openhands/qwen3-coder-480b-a35b-instruct
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
' AwesomeMusic - System Architecture
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam linetype ortho
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam wrapWidth 200

title AwesomeMusic - System Architecture

rectangle "External" as External {
  component "Browser" as Browser
}

node "VM Cluster (4 VMs)" as Cluster {
  frame "DMZ / Edge" as DMZ {
    component "Nginx 1.19.8\n(HTTPS Gateway)" as Nginx
  }

  package "Application Layer" as App {
    component "Web Frontend\n(HTML/CSS/JS, Bootstrap)" as Frontend
    component "Web Backend\n(Golang, Gin)" as Backend
    component "Scheduler" as Scheduler
    component "Algorithm Service" as Algo
  }

  package "Infrastructure Layer" as Infra {
    component "RabbitMQ 3.8.14\n(Task Queue)" as MQ
    component "etcd 3.4.15\n(Config/Service Registry)" as Etcd
    component "Hadoop 3.2.2\n(HDFS/YARN)" as Hadoop
    database "Database\n(Data Storage)" as DB
  }
}

' External access and gateway
Browser -[#blue]-> Nginx : HTTPS
Nginx -[#blue]-> Frontend : static content
Nginx -[#blue]-> Backend : reverse proxy (HTTPS/TCP)

' Frontend-backend interaction
Frontend -[#green]-> Backend : REST/HTTPS

' Backend data and infra dependencies
Backend -[#brown]-> DB : CRUD
Backend -[#gray]-> Etcd : config/coordination

' Scheduling and queues
Scheduler -[#orange]-> MQ : enqueue daily tasks (02:00)
Scheduler -[#teal]-> Backend : poll task status
Scheduler -[#gray]-> Etcd : config/coordination

' Generation requests via queue
Backend -[#orange]-> MQ : enqueue generation tasks

' Algorithm workers consume tasks and produce results
Algo -[#orange]-> MQ : consume tasks / publish status
Algo -[#brown]-> DB : models/labels/recommendations
Algo -[#purple]-> Hadoop : training/classification data I/O
Algo -[#gray]-> Etcd : config/coordination

' Optional status updates via MQ back to backend

samples.pred_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

actor "API Client" as Client
actor "Platform Operator" as Operator

package "Turing" {
  component "API Server" as APIServer {
    [HTTP Handlers\n(OpenAPI 3.0.3)] as Handlers
    [Authorization Middleware] as AuthMW
    [Request Validation\n(OpenAPI)] as ValMW
    [Router Deployer] as Deployer
  }
}

cloud "Kubernetes Cluster" as K8s {
  [Kubernetes API] as K8sAPI
  node "Router Deployment(s)" as RouterDepl {
    [Turing Router] as Router
  }
  node "PyFuncEnsembler Service" as EnsemblerSvc
}

cloud "Jaeger" as Jaeger
database "MLflow Model Registry" as MLflow
component "Local Image Build\n(PyFuncEnsembler)" as ImageBuild

Client --> Handlers : HTTP/JSON\nRouters & Ensemblers APIs
Handlers -down-> AuthMW : Authorization checks\n(if enabled)
Handlers -down-> ValMW : Request validation\n(if enabled)
Deployer --> K8sAPI : Create/Update/Delete\nRouter Deployments (FR-007)

Router --> EnsemblerSvc : HTTP\nEnsembler requests (FR-006)
Router ..> Jaeger : Tracing spans\n(if enabled/sampled) (NFR-002)

ImageBuild --> MLflow : Download model\nartifacts (C-003)
ImageBuild ..> EnsemblerSvc : Builds local Docker image\nfor runtime (FR-006)

Operator --> K8sAPI : Cluster operations\n(as needed)

note right of Handlers
- Endpoints:
  - GET /projects/{project_id}/routers (FR-001)
  - Ensembler CRUD (FR-002..FR-005)
- JSON responses for Ensembler operations (DR-002, DR-008, DR-009)
- Path params: project_id, ensembler_id (DR-004, DR-005)
end note

note right of APIServer
- API constrained to OpenAPI-defined endpoints and schemas; OpenAPI 3.0.3 (C-001, NFR-004)
- Middleware mounting controlled by configuration (NFR-001)
end note

note right of K8sAPI
- Uses Kubernetes interfaces for router deployments (FR-007)
- Constraint: Kubernetes environment required (C-002)
end note

note right of Router
- Default port 8080; override via ports.containerPort (NFR-003, C-004)
- Router protocol: HTTP_JSON or UPI_V1 (DR-007)
- Timeout pattern: ^[0-9]+(ms|s|m|h)$ (DR-006)
- Jaeger tracing integrated; sampling controls traces (NFR-002)
end note

note right of EnsemblerSvc
- Exposes user-defined ensemblers as a webservice (FR-006)
- Docker-based runtime; image built from MLflow artifacts (C-003)
end note
@enduml

@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #555555
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "Turing User" as TuringUser
rectangle "Router Clients" as RouterClients
rectangle "Router Endpoints" as RouterEndpoints

package "Turing System" as TuringSystem {
    rectangle "Turing UI\nWeb interface to configure Routers" as TuringUi
    rectangle "Turing API Server\nHTTP REST API to configure Routers" as TuringApiServer
    database "Database\nPersistent storage for Router objects" as RouterDatabase
}

rectangle "Secret Store" as SecretStore
rectangle "Authorization Service" as AuthorizationService

package "Kubernetes Cluster" as KubernetesCluster {
    rectangle "Knative Controller" as KnativeController
    rectangle "Istio" as Istio
}

rectangle "Experimentation Service" as ExperimentationService
rectangle "Machine Learning Platform\nMLP API Server" as MlpApiServer
rectangle "Monitoring and Alerting Service" as MonitoringService

TuringUser --> TuringUi : use
TuringUi --> TuringApiServer : make HTTP calls
TuringApiServer --> RouterDatabase : save and retrieve routers

RouterClients --> RouterEndpoints : requests from router clients
TuringApiServer --> RouterEndpoints : create endpoints\nfor accessing routers

TuringApiServer --> SecretStore : save and retrieve\ncredentials
TuringApiServer --> AuthorizationService : check user\npermissions
TuringApiServer --> KubernetesCluster : deploy router\nworkloads
TuringApiServer --> ExperimentationService : retrieve experiments\nconfigured for routers
TuringApiServer --> MlpApiServer : retrieve projects and\nenvironments for routers
TuringApiServer --> MonitoringService : configure alerts based\non router metrics

KubernetesCluster --> KnativeController : manage router services
KubernetesCluster --> Istio : expose router traffic
Istio --> RouterEndpoints : route client traffic
MonitoringService --> KubernetesCluster : retrieve router metrics
@enduml

samples.title: s000045_597bf972 / direct/claude-sonnet-4-6
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
!define RECTANGLE class

skinparam backgroundColor #FAFAFA
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam defaultFontSize 12
skinparam defaultFontName Arial

skinparam component {
  BackgroundColor #DDEEFF
  BorderColor #336699
  FontColor #003366
}

skinparam node {
  BackgroundColor #FFF8DC
  BorderColor #CC9900
}

skinparam package {
  BackgroundColor #F0F8E8
  BorderColor #559944
  FontColor #224411
}

skinparam rectangle {
  BackgroundColor #EEF0FF
  BorderColor #5566AA
}

skinparam database {
  BackgroundColor #FFE8E8
  BorderColor #AA3333
}

skinparam cloud {
  BackgroundColor #E8F8FF
  BorderColor #2277AA
}

skinparam arrow {
  Color #444444
  FontSize 10
}

title QAPM Android SDK — System Architecture Diagram\n(Background Scheduling · Upload Processing · HTTP Instrumentation)

node "Android Host Application" as HostApp {
  component "Application Code" as AppCode
  component "QAPM SDK Integrator\n(Initialization / Config)" as Integrator
}

package "QAPM SDK Core" as QAPMCore {

  package "Background Scheduling Layer" as SchedLayer {
    component "QAPM Handler Manager\n(FR-010, DR-001, DR-002)" as HandlerMgr {
      component "Default Background\nHandlerThread + Handler\n(FR-001, FR-002, NFR-001)" as BgHandler
      component "Main-Thread Handler\n(Looper.getMainLooper)\n(FR-010, DR-002)" as MainHandler
    }
    component "Work Handler Init\n(FR-001, FR-002)" as WorkHandlerInit
  }

  package "Upload Processing Layer" as UploadLayer {
    component "Upload Scheduler\n(FR-002, FR-003)" as UploadScheduler
    component "Network Connectivity\nGate\n(FR-003, NFR-002, C-003)" as NetworkGate
    component "Forced-Send / Storage\nPop Handler\n(FR-004)" as ForcedSendHandler
    component "Upload File Processor\n(FR-005, FR-006, FR-007\nDR-003..DR-008, NFR-005)" as UploadFileProcessor
    component "Upload Success / Failure\nCallback Handler\n(FR-007, DR-008)" as SendCallbackHandler
  }

  package "Configuration Management" as ConfigMgmt {
    component "QAPM Config Manager\n(AD-003)" as ConfigMgr
    component "Configured Data Sender\n(FR-006, DR-007)" as DataSender
  }

  package "Apache HttpClient Instrumentation Layer" as HttpInstrLayer {
    component "Instrumented Response\nHandler Wrapper\n(FR-008, DR-009)" as ResponseHandlerWrapper
    component "Transaction State\nInspector / Instrumentor\n(FR-008, DR-009)" as TxStateInspector
    component "Instrumented Response\nEntity Wrapper\n(FR-009, DR-010, DR-011\nNFR-003)" as ResponseEntityWrapper
    component "Counting Input Stream\n(DR-010, FR-009)" as CountingInputStream
  }
}

package "Android Platform Services" as AndroidPlatform {
  component "Handler / HandlerThread\n/ Looper\n(C-001, NFR-004)" as AndroidHandlerAPI
  component "Android Context\n(FR-006, DR-006, C-001)" as AndroidContext
  component "Network Connectivity\nService\n(FR-003, AD-001)" as NetworkService
}

package "Local File Storage" as LocalStorage {
  database "Upload Directory\n/ Upload Files\n(DR-003, DR-004, AD-002)" as UploadDir
  database "Pending Stored Data\n(FR-004)" as PendingStore
}

cloud "Apache HttpClient\nAbstractions\n(C-002, NFR-004)" as ApacheHttpClient {
  component "ResponseHandler<T>" as ApacheRH
  component "HttpResponse" as ApacheHttpResp
  component "HttpEntity" as ApacheHttpEntity
}

cloud "Remote Upload Endpoint\n(AD-005, outside SRS scope)" as RemoteEndpoint

' ---- Host App connections ----
AppCode --> Integrator : uses / initializes
Integrator --> HandlerMgr : initializes
Integrator --> ConfigMgr : configures

' ---- Scheduling Layer internal ----
HandlerMgr --> WorkHandlerInit : creates HandlerThread,\nstarts, binds looper
WorkHandlerInit --> BgHandler : creates work handler\nfrom background looper
BgHandler --> AndroidHandlerAPI : uses Handler /\nHandlerThread / Looper
MainHandler --> AndroidHandlerAPI : uses main Looper

' ---- Upload scheduling ----
AppCode --> UploadScheduler : posts upload request\n(FR-002)
UploadScheduler --> BgHandler : post runnable/upload\nto background handler (FR-002)
BgHandler --> NetworkGate : triggers upload\nprocessing

' ---- Upload processing flow ----
NetworkGate --> NetworkService : check connectivity\n(FR-003)
NetworkGate --> ForcedSendHandler : if forced-send flag set\n(FR-004)
ForcedSendHandler --> PendingStore : pop stored data\n(FR-004)
NetworkGate --> UploadFileProcessor : if network available\n(FR-003, FR-005)
UploadFileProcessor --> UploadDir : enumerate upload files,\nread bParam (FR-005, DR-003..DR-005)
UploadFileProcessor --> AndroidContext : derive cParam\n(FR-006, DR-006)
UploadFileProcessor --> DataSender : submit bParam + cParam\n(FR-006, DR-007)
DataSender --> SendCallbackHandler : success / failure\ncallback (FR-007)
SendCallbackHandler --> UploadDir : delete file on success\n(FR-007, DR-008)
SendCallbackHandler --> HandlerMgr : log failure\n(FR-007, DR-008)

' ---- Config ----
ConfigMgr --> DataSender : provides configured sender\n(AD-003)
DataSender --> RemoteEndpoint : send bParam / cParam pair

' ---- HTTP Instrumentation ----
AppCode --> ResponseHandlerWrapper : wraps ResponseHandler\n(FR-008, C-002)
ResponseHandlerWrapper --> TxStateInspector : inspect / instrument\ntransaction state (FR-008, DR-009)
ResponseHandlerWrapper --> ApacheRH : delegate handleResponse()\nto wrapped handler (FR-008)
ApacheHttpResp --> ResponseHandlerWrapper : HttpResponse input\n(FR-008)
TxStateInspector --> ResponseHandlerWrapper : instrumented transaction\nstate result (DR-009)

AppCode --> ResponseEntityWrapper : wraps HttpEntity\n(FR-009, NFR-003)
ResponseEntityWrapper --> ApacheHttpEntity : delegates writeTo()\n(FR-009, DR-011)
ResponseEntityWrapper --> CountingInputStream : getContent() returns\nor reuses counting stream\n(FR-009, DR-010)
CountingInputStream --> ApacheHttpEntity : reads underlying\nentity content (DR-010)

' ---- Android platform links ----
UploadFileProcessor --> AndroidContext : Android Context API\n(C-001)
NetworkGate --> NetworkService : Android connectivity\nservice (C-001)
ResponseHandlerWrapper --> ApacheHttpClient : Apache HttpClient\nabstraction (C-002)
ResponseEntityWrapper --> ApacheHttpClient : Apache HttpClient\nabstraction (C-002)

@enduml
samples.gt_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #333333

    BorderColor #226655
    BorderThickness 1

skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFF99
    BorderColor #226655
    BorderThickness 1
}

rectangle "Application" as Application #99FF66

package "QAPM_SDK" as QapmSdkPackage {
    rectangle "QAPM-SDK" as QapmSdk
    rectangle "QAPM_Collect" as QapmCollect
    rectangle "QAPM_Storage" as QapmStorage
    rectangle "QAPM_Report" as QapmReport
}

package "QAPM_Plugin" as QapmPluginPackage {
    rectangle "QAPM_Plugin" as QapmPlugin #66CCCC
    rectangle "Plugin init compile" as PluginInitCompile #66CCCC
}

Application --> QapmSdk : Import QAPM_SDK
QapmSdk --> QapmCollect : API
QapmSdk --> QapmStorage : API
QapmSdk --> QapmReport : API

Application --> QapmPlugin : apply QAPM_Plugin
QapmPlugin --> PluginInitCompile : initialize and compile
@enduml

completeness.reasoning: The architecture diagram successfully represents all the major components, interactions, and external dependencies specified in the SRS. Key functional areas like background scheduling, the multi-step upload process (including network gating, forced send, and file processing), and Apache HttpClient instrumentation are all modeled with distinct components. It even correctly depicts elements that are explicitly out-of-scope (e.g., `Remote Upload Endpoint`), demonstrating a full understanding of the system's boundaries. The few requirements not explicitly tagged (AD-004, C-004) are nevertheless implicitly represented by the structure and context of the diagram, resulting in a comprehensive model.

faithfulness.reasoning: The diagram accurately translates the specified requirements into architectural components and interactions. The flow of control, particularly in the complex upload processing logic, faithfully mirrors the sequence of operations described in the functional requirements (FR-003 to FR-007). Similarly, the delegation patterns for the HttpClient wrappers (FR-008, FR-009) and the asynchronous dispatch model (FR-002, NFR-001) are correctly depicted. The architecture does not misrepresent or contradict any specified behavior.

architectural_rationality.reasoning: The architecture demonstrates strong reasoning by explicitly addressing the key quality attributes (ASRs). Non-functional requirements directly shape the design: the `Background Scheduling Layer` is a direct response to performance requirement NFR-001; the `Network Connectivity Gate` implements reliability requirement NFR-002; and the modular decomposition of the `Upload Processing Layer` supports the maintainability requirement NFR-005 for verifiable steps. The logical layering promotes a clear separation of concerns.

traceability.reasoning: The diagram provides outstanding traceability by embedding requirement identifiers (FR, NFR, DR, C, AD) directly onto most components and interactions. This practice creates an explicit and verifiable link between the requirements specification and the architectural design, making it simple for a reviewer to confirm that a specific requirement, such as FR-004 (Forced-send handling), is mapped to a specific component (`Forced-Send / Storage Pop Handler`). This extensive annotation covers the vast majority of requirements.

readability.reasoning: The diagram is exceptionally readable due to its logical grouping of components into layers, consistent use of color and style, and clear, descriptive labels on components and connectors. The layout is uncluttered, and the title explicitly defines the diagram's scope. @startuml
!define RECTANGLE class

title System Architecture - AI-Driven Text Adventure Game and Community Platform

skinparam componentStyle rectangle

package "Frontend Layer" {
  [Vue.js/Nuxt.js Web Application] as frontend
}

package "API Gateway & Load Balancer" {
  [API Gateway] as gateway
}

package "Backend Services" {
  [Iris Web Framework] as backend
  
  package "Core Services" {
    [User Management Service] as user_service
    [Game Service] as game_service
    [Community Service] as community_service
    [Administration Service] as admin_service
    [Notification Service] as notification_service
    [Favorites Service] as favorites_service
  }
  
  backend --> user_service : handles
  backend --> game_service : handles
  backend --> community_service : handles
  backend --> admin_service : handles
  backend --> notification_service : handles
  backend --> favorites_service : handles
}

package "Data Layer" {
  [GORM ORM] as orm
  [MySQL Database] as database
  
  orm --> database : manages
}

package "External Services" {
  [Large Language Model Provider] as llm_provider
}

game_service --> llm_provider : invokes
backend --> orm : uses
user_service --> orm : uses
community_service --> orm : uses
admin_service --> orm : uses
notification_service --> orm : uses
favorites_service --> orm : uses

frontend --> gateway : connects to
gateway --> backend : routes to

note right of frontend
  Client-side application
  built with Vue.js and Nuxt.js
end note

note right of backend
  Server-side application
  built with Iris framework
end note

note right of database
  Relational database
  managed by MySQL
end note

note right of llm_provider
  External AI service
  for dynamic content generation
end note

note top of user_service
  Handles:
  - Registration/Login
  - Profile management
  - Account operations
end note

note top of game_service
  Handles:
  - Game saves
  - Background setup
  - Choice progression
  - Save sharing
end note

note top of community_service
  Handles:
  - Posts
  - Comments/Replies
  - Search
  - Recommendations
end note

note top of admin_service
  Handles:
  - User governance
  - Post review
  - Reports handling
end note

note top of notification_service
  Handles:
  - Comment/reply notifications
  - Review status updates
end note

note top of favorites_service
  Handles:
  - Post favoriting
  - Favorites management
end note

package "Infrastructure" {
  [Docker Containers] as docker
  [Kubernetes Orchestration] as k8s
  
  k8s --> docker : orchestrates
  docker --> backend : contains
  docker --> database : contains
}

@enduml

@startuml
' Directional setting (Top to Down)
top to bottom direction

' Styling for different layers
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor<<Frontend>> #e1f5fe
    BorderColor<<Frontend>> #01579b
    
    BackgroundColor<<Presentation>> #f3e5f5
    BorderColor<<Presentation>> #4a148c
    
    BackgroundColor<<Backend>> #fff3e0
    BorderColor<<Backend>> #e65100
}

skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor<<Storage>> #e8f5e9
    BorderColor<<Storage>> #1b5e20
}

' Layers / Subgraphs
package "Frontend Layer" {
    rectangle "React Framework" as React <<Frontend>>
    rectangle "React-Router: Page Navigation" as Router <<Frontend>>
    rectangle "Netron: Node Drawing Features" as NetronFE <<Frontend>>
}

package "Data Presentation Layer" {
    rectangle "RESTful API Design" as RESTAPI <<Presentation>>
    rectangle "Netron: Graph Representation Language" as GraphLang <<Presentation>>
}

package "Backend Layer" {
    rectangle "Flask: Backend Framework" as Flask <<Backend>>
    rectangle "SQLAlchemy: ORM Framework" as SQLAlchemy <<Backend>>
    rectangle "Netron: Model Conversion & Parsing" as NetronBE <<Backend>>
}

package "Storage / Database Layer" {
    database "MySQL: Relational Database" as MySQL <<Storage>>
    database "Neo4j: Graph Database" as Neo4j <<Storage>>
}

React --> NetronFE
React --> RESTAPI

samples.title: s000351_ed818cab / metagpt-custom/deepseek-v3.2
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
' Refined Architecture for Kangrand ECDLP Solver

package "Input/Output Layer" {
    component "InputFileParser" as InputParser
    component "ConfigurationHandler" as ConfigHandler
    component "ResultFileGenerator" as ResultGenerator
    component "WorkFileManager" as WorkFiles
}

package "Orchestration Layer" {
    component "CommandLineInterface" as CLI
    component "DistributedServer" as Server
    component "DistributedClient" as Client
    component "OfflineMerger" as Merger
}

package "Core Solver Layer" {
    component "SolverEngine" as Solver {
        component "DistinguishedPointManager" as DPointManager
        component "KangarooStateManager" as StateManager
        component "WsplitBackupManager" as BackupManager
    }
}

node "GPU Hardware" as GPU

' Input/Output Layer dependencies
InputParser --> ConfigHandler : reads configuration
InputParser --> WorkFiles : writes parsed input
ConfigHandler --> WorkFiles : manages config files
WorkFiles --> ResultGenerator : provides data for results

' Orchestration Layer dependencies
CLI --> InputParser : invokes parsing
CLI --> ConfigHandler : loads configuration
CLI --> Server : starts/stops
CLI --> Client : configures/launches
CLI --> Merger : invokes merge
CLI --> Solver : configures local solving

Server --> ConfigHandler : reads server config
Server --> Solver : coordinates solving work
Server --> WorkFiles : generates work files
Server --> Client : distributes work

Client --> ConfigHandler : reads client config
Client --> Solver : performs computation
Client --> Server : requests work/reports results
Client --> WorkFiles : reads/writes work files

Merger --> WorkFiles : reads work files
Merger --> Solver : checks for solved keys
Merger --> ResultGenerator : outputs final results

' Core Solver Layer dependencies
Solver --> InputParser : reads problem definition
Solver --> DPointManager : manages distinguished points
Solver --> StateManager : persists kangaroo states
Solver --> BackupManager : handles wsplit backups
Solver --> WorkFiles : periodic save/load (FR-004)
Solver --> GPU : optional acceleration

' Component relationships within Solver
DPointManager --> StateManager : updates states
StateManager --> BackupManager : triggers backups
BackupManager --> WorkFiles : creates prefixed backups

' External hardware dependency
Solver ..> GPU : optional hardware acceleration

' Data flow annotations
note right of InputParser
  Parses ASCII input files
  with hex values and
  public keys (FR-001, FR-002)
end note

note right of WorkFiles
  Portable across hardware/
  configurations (NFR-005)
  Periodic saves (FR-004)
end note

note right of Solver
  Internal memory management

@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #5A7FA3
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "Kangrand Distributed Pollard Kangaroo" as Kangrand {
    package "Distributed Search Group A" as SearchGroupA {
        rectangle "Server\n-wsplit" as ServerA
        rectangle "Client A1" as ClientA1
        rectangle "Client A2" as ClientA2
        rectangle "Client A3" as ClientA3
        database "Kangaroo file A1" as KangarooFileA1
        database "Kangaroo file A2" as KangarooFileA2
        database "Kangaroo file A3" as KangarooFileA3
        database "Work files\nHashTable A" as WorkFilesA
        database "Result.txt A" as ResultA
    }

    package "Distributed Search Group B" as SearchGroupB {
        rectangle "Server\n-wsplit" as ServerB
        rectangle "Client B1" as ClientB1
        rectangle "Client B2" as ClientB2
        rectangle "Client B3" as ClientB3
        database "Kangaroo file B1" as KangarooFileB1
        database "Kangaroo file B2" as KangarooFileB2
        database "Kangaroo file B3" as KangarooFileB3
        database "Work files\nHashTable B" as WorkFilesB
        database "Result.txt B" as ResultB
    }

    rectangle "Merger" as Merger
    database "Master WorkFile\nHashTable" as MasterWorkFile
}

ServerA --> ClientA1 : distribute range work
ServerA --> ClientA2 : distribute range work
ServerA --> ClientA3 : distribute range work
ClientA1 --> KangarooFileA1 : save kangaroo state
ClientA2 --> KangarooFileA2 : save kangaroo state
ClientA3 --> KangarooFileA3 : save kangaroo state
ServerA --> WorkFilesA : wsplit backups\nreset hash table
ServerA --> ResultA : solved key output

ServerB --> ClientB1 : distribute range work
ServerB --> ClientB2 : distribute range work
ServerB --> ClientB3 : distribute range work
ClientB1 --> KangarooFileB1 : save kangaroo state
ClientB2 --> KangarooFileB2 : save kangaroo state
ClientB3 --> KangarooFileB3 : save kangaroo state
ServerB --> WorkFilesB : wsplit backups\nreset hash table
ServerB --> ResultB : solved key output

ServerA .. ServerB : parallel distributed search
WorkFilesA --> Merger : merge work files
WorkFilesB --> Merger : merge work files
ResultA --> Merger : collision result
ResultB --> Merger : collision result
Merger --> MasterWorkFile : master work file\nmerged hash table
@enduml

samples.title: ModelSharePlatform / openhands/claude-sonnet-4-6
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
title Deep Learning Model Sharing Platform - System Architecture

skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false

' Users and client
package "Users" {
  rectangle "General User" as GeneralUser
  rectangle "Administrator" as AdminUser
}

node "Web Browser (SPA)" as Browser

' Presentation and backend
component "Web Frontend\n(Vue + TypeScript)\n• i18n (CN/EN)\n• UI/UX" as Frontend
component "Application Backend\n(Spring Boot)" as Backend

' Core application modules
package "Application Modules" as Modules {
  component "Account Module\n• Registration/Login\n• Password Recovery\n• Profile & Logout\n• AuthN/AuthZ" as Account
  component "Q&A Module\n• Questions\n• Answers" as QA
  component "Blog Module\n• Articles\n• Comments" as Blog
  component "Model Module\n• Upload/Review Entry\n• Download/Modify/Delete\n• Online Invocation\n• Recommendation Input\n• Purchase" as Model
  component "Dataset Module\n• Upload/Download\n• Modify/Delete" as Dataset
  component "Follow Module\n• Follow/Unfollow" as Follow
  component "Recommendation Module\n• Personalized Model Pool" as Recommend
  component "Admin Governance Module\n• Account Cancellation\n• Illegal Content Deletion" as AdminGov
}

' Data and external systems
database "MySQL\n(Relational Data)" as DB
cloud "Object Storage\n(Model & Dataset Files)" as ObjStore
cloud "Email Service\n(Password Recovery)" as Email
cloud "Payment Provider\n(External)" as Payment
node "Model Execution Sandbox\n(Online Invocation Runtime)" as Runtime

' Client interactions
GeneralUser --> Browser
AdminUser --> Browser
Browser --> Frontend
Frontend --> Backend

' Backend to modules
Backend --> Account
Backend --> QA
Backend --> Blog
Backend --> Model
Backend --> Dataset
Backend --> Follow
Backend --> Recommend
Backend --> AdminGov

' Modules to data stores
Account --> DB
QA --> DB
Blog --> DB
Model --> DB
Dataset --> DB
Follow --> DB
Recommend --> DB
AdminGov --> DB

' Binary file storage
Model --> ObjStore
Dataset --> ObjStore

' External integrations
Account --> Email : "Send reset email"
Model --> Runtime : "Run model"
Model --> Payment : "Purchase processing"

' Notes reflecting SRS constraints and assumptions
note right of Recommend
Uses browsing history and registration information
to compute recommended models (per AD-003).
end note

note bottom of Payment
Payment channel unspecified in SRS (AD-005);
represented as an external provider.
end note

note bottom of Runtime
Executes selected pretrained models using platform
or user-uploaded datasets; returns results.
end note

note top of Backend
Security, validation, and consistency checks;
enforces admin privileges and content moderation.
end note

@enduml

@startuml
' --- Configuration & Styles ---
skinparam topboxSelection true
skinparam Direction top to bottom
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false

' Define Styles based on original ClassDefs
<style>
    .client {
        BackgroundColor #e1f5fe
        LineColor #01579b
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .service {
        BackgroundColor #e8f5e9
        LineColor #2e7d32
        LineThickness 2
    }
    .db {
        BackgroundColor #fff9c4
        LineColor #fbc02d
        LineThickness 2
    }
</style>

' --- Client Layer ---
package "Browser / Client Layer" as Browser_Layer {
    rectangle "Frontend Interface\n(Bootstrap-Vue)" as FE_Interface <<client>>
}

' --- Server Layer ---
package "Server Layer\n(Spring Boot)" as Server_Layer {
    
    package "Core Modules" as Core_Modules {
        ' Layout is TB within the package
        rectangle "Account Management Module" as AccountMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Q&A Community Module" as QAMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Blog Sharing Module" as BlogMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Model Management Module" as ModelMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Dataset Management Module" as DatasetMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Following Management Module" as FollowMod <<service>>
        rectangle "Admin Module" as AdminMod <<service>>
    }
}

' --- Database Layer ---
package "Database Layer\n(MySQL)" as Database_Layer {
    database "User Data" as UserDB <<db>>
    database "Q&A Community Data" as QADB <<db>>
    database "Blog Data" as BlogDB <<db>>
    database "Model Data" as ModelDB <<db>>
    database "Dataset Data" as DatasetDB <<db>>
    database "Following Data" as FollowDB <<db>>
}

' --- Relationships ---
FE_Interface --> Core_Modules
Core_Modules --> Database_Layer

@enduml

samples.title: s000061_2eb4d99c / mini-swe-agent/claude-sonnet-4-6
samples.pred_source:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam rectangle {
  BackgroundColor<<layer>> LightSteelBlue
  BorderColor<<layer>> DarkSlateBlue
}

title AWS IoT Device Simulator - Architecture Diagram

actor "Administrator / Operator" as Admin
actor "API Client" as APIClient
actor "AWS Deployment Workflow" as DeployWorkflow

rectangle "Presentation Layer" <<layer>> {
  [Web Management Console] as WebConsole
  [Amazon CloudFront / S3 Hosting] as CDN
}

rectangle "API Layer" <<layer>> {
  [Amazon API Gateway\n(Regional REST API, stage: prod)] as APIGW
  note right of APIGW
    - CORS: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS
    - Request Validation (params + body)
    - Access Logging (JSON, INFO level)
    - X-Ray Tracing enabled
  end note
}

rectangle "Application / Service Layer" <<layer>> {
  [Simulation Management\nLambda Service] as SimMgmtLambda
  [Device Type Management\nLambda Service] as DevTypeLambda
  [Device Simulator\nLambda / Execution Engine] as SimExecLambda
  [Route Loader\nLambda Service] as RouteLoaderLambda
  [Custom Resource Handler\nLambda] as CustomResourceLambda
}

rectangle "Orchestration Layer" <<layer>> {
  [AWS Step Functions\n(Simulation Workflow)] as StepFunctions
}

rectangle "Data & Storage Layer" <<layer>> {
  database "Amazon DynamoDB\n(Simulations & Device Types)" as DynamoDB
  storage "Amazon S3\n(Route Data: ROUTE_BUCKET)" as S3Routes
  storage "Amazon S3\n(Web Console Hosting)" as S3Console
}

rectangle "Messaging & IoT Layer" <<layer>> {
  [AWS IoT Core\n(IoT Topics)] as IoTCore
  [Amazon CloudWatch Logs\n(API Access Logs)] as CloudWatchLogs
}

rectangle "Identity & Security Layer" <<layer>> {
  [Amazon Cognito\n(Identity & Auth)] as Cognito
}

rectangle "Deployment Layer" <<layer>> {
  [AWS CloudFormation\n(Stack Deployment)] as CloudFormation
}

' User interactions
Admin --> CDN : HTTPS
APIClient --> APIGW : REST API (HTTPS)
DeployWorkflow --> CloudFormation : Stack deployment

' Presentation layer
CDN --> S3Console : serve static assets
WebConsole --> APIGW : REST API calls

' API to Application layer
APIGW --> SimMgmtLambda : invoke
APIGW --> DevTypeLambda : invoke
APIGW --> SimExecLambda : invoke
APIGW --> Cognito : auth/authorization

' Application to Orchestration
SimMgmtLambda --> StepFunctions : start/manage simulation workflows

' Step Functions to Execution
StepFunctions --> SimExecLambda : orchestrate simulation steps
StepFunctions --> RouteLoaderLambda : trigger route loading

' Application to Data layer
SimMgmtLambda --> DynamoDB : read/write simulation records
DevTypeLambda --> DynamoDB : read/write device type records
SimExecLambda --> DynamoDB : read simulation state

' Route Loading
RouteLoaderLambda --> S3Routes : GetObject (ROUTE_BUCKET / route key)

' IoT Publishing
SimExecLambda --> IoTCore : publish simulated device messages

' Logging
APIGW --> CloudWatchLogs : JSON access logs

' Custom Resource
CloudFormation --> CustomResourceLambda : invoke on deploy/update/delete
CustomResourceLambda --> S3Routes : initialize route bucket resources

@enduml

@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #4A5D78
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam cloud {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "AWS IoT Device Simulator" as IotDeviceSimulator {
    rectangle "Users" as Users
    rectangle "Browser Web UI\nstatic app client" as BrowserWebUi
    cloud "Amazon CloudFront" as CloudFront
    database "Amazon S3\nWeb UI bucket" as WebUiBucket
    rectangle "Amazon Cognito" as Cognito
    rectangle "Amazon API Gateway\nREST API" as ApiGateway
    rectangle "AWS Lambda\nSimulator API" as LambdaSimulator
    database "Amazon DynamoDB\nSimulator state" as DynamoDb
    database "Amazon S3\nRoute data bucket" as RouteBucket
    rectangle "AWS Step Functions\nSimulation workflow" as StepFunctions
    cloud "AWS IoT Core" as IotCore
    rectangle "AWS CloudFormation\nCustom resources" as CloudFormation
}

Users --> CloudFront : web app request
CloudFront --> WebUiBucket : fetch web assets
CloudFront --> BrowserWebUi : deliver static web UI
BrowserWebUi --> Cognito : authenticate
Cognito --> BrowserWebUi : auth tokens
BrowserWebUi --> ApiGateway : API requests
ApiGateway --> LambdaSimulator : REST integration
LambdaSimulator <--> DynamoDb : simulator metadata and state
LambdaSimulator --> RouteBucket : get route JSON\nby routeName
RouteBucket --> LambdaSimulator : route JSON
LambdaSimulator --> StepFunctions : start simulation workflow
StepFunctions --> IotCore : publish simulated device messages
CloudFormation --> WebUiBucket : deploy/configure\nsolution assets
CloudFormation --> LambdaSimulator : custom-resource setup
@enduml

samples.pred_source:
@startuml
title MTProto Server Architecture - Component & Deployment Diagram

' Deployment Layer
node "Deployment Context" {
    [MTProto Server] as mtproto #lightblue
    [MySQL] as mysql_db #lightgray
    [Redis] as redis_db #lightgray
    [etcd] as etcd_db #lightgray
    [Kafka] as kafka_mq #lightgray
    [MinIO] as minio_store #lightgray
}

note right of mtproto
  Can be deployed in:
  - Docker Container
  - Manual Binary
end note

note right of mysql_db
  Can be:
  - MySQL Container
  - External MySQL
end note

' Component Layer
package "Client Access Layer" {
    component [MTProto Server] as mtproto_comp
}

package "Service Layer" {
    component [Authorization Service] as auth_svc
    component [Session Service] as session_svc
    component [Salt Service] as salt_svc
    component [Registration Service] as register_svc
    component [Service Message Handler] as svcmsg_svc
    component [Media Processing Service] as media_svc
    component [Future Salt Service] as futuresalt_svc
    component [Verification Code Service] as verifycode_svc
}

package "Storage Layer" {
    component [Database Adapters] as db_adapters
    component [Object Storage Client] as obj_client
}

package "Infrastructure Layer" {
    component [Message Queue Client] as mq_client
}

' Storage Systems
database [MySQL] as mysql_db_sys
database [Redis] as redis_db_sys
database [etcd] as etcd_db_sys
cloud [MinIO] as minio_cloud_sys
cloud [Kafka] as kafka_cloud_sys

' Connections
mtproto_comp --> auth_svc : uses
auth_svc --> session_svc : creates/manages sessions
session_svc --> salt_svc : requests salts
register_svc --> session_svc : uses session context
svcmsg_svc --> session_svc : manages session state
auth_svc --> db_adapters : stores auth data
session_svc --> db_adapters : stores session data
salt_svc --> db_adapters : stores salt data
register_svc --> db_adapters : stores registration data
svcmsg_svc --> db_adapters : stores service msg data
media_svc --> db_adapters : stores media metadata
db_adapters --> mysql_db_sys : persists data
db_adapters --> redis_db_sys : caches data
db_adapters --> etcd_db_sys : stores metadata
obj_client --> minio_cloud_sys : stores objects
mq_client --> kafka_cloud_sys : sends/receives messages
mtproto_comp --> mq_client : async notifications
auth_svc --> mq_client : async tasks
session_svc --> mq_client : async events
register_svc --> mq_client : verification events
svcmsg_svc --> mq_client : session cleanup events
salt_svc --> mq_client : future salt events
futuresalt_svc --> salt_svc : provides future salts
verifycode_svc ..> register_svc : <<external>>\nverifies codes
media_svc ..> svcmsg_svc : handles rpc_drop_answer
media_svc ..> session_svc : handles destroy_session

note top of media_svc
  Uses external tools:\n- FFmpeg\n- FFProbe
end note

@enduml
samples.gt_source:

skinparam componentStyle rectangle
left to right direction

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam ArrowColor #34495E
skinparam ArrowThickness 1.5
skinparam package {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam rectangle {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam database {
    BackgroundColor #FFF2D8
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}
skinparam queue {
    BackgroundColor #FFFFFF
    BorderColor #555555
    BorderThickness 1
}

package "Clients" as Clients {
    rectangle "iOS" as IosClient
    rectangle "Android" as AndroidClient
    rectangle "Web" as WebClient
    rectangle "PC" as PcClient
}

package "Access Layer" as AccessLayer {
    rectangle "Gateway" as Gateway
    rectangle "Auth Session" as AuthSession
    rectangle "Session" as Session
    rectangle "Status" as Status
}

rectangle "BFF" as Bff

package "Services" as ServiceLayer {
    rectangle "DFS" as Dfs
    rectangle "Services" as Services
    rectangle "Msg" as Msg
}

package "Storages" as Storages {
    database "Redis" as Redis
    database "MySQL" as Mysql
    database "MinIO" as Minio
}

rectangle "Push" as Push
rectangle "Sync" as Sync
queue "Kafka" as Kafka

IosClient <--> Gateway : tcp
AndroidClient <--> Gateway : tcp
WebClient <--> Gateway : websocket
PcClient <--> Gateway : websocket

Gateway <--> Session : session routing
Session --> AuthSession : auth state
Session --> Status : presence status
Session --> Redis : session state
Session --> Bff : rpc

Bff --> Dfs : file rpc
Bff --> Services : business rpc
Bff --> Msg : message rpc
Bff --> Kafka : publish async events
Sync --> Gateway : rpc

Services --> Redis : cache data
Services --> Mysql : persistent data
Dfs --> Minio : object storage
Msg --> Kafka : publish message events
Kafka --> Sync : message stream
Sync --> Push : push delivery
@enduml

@startuml
!define RECTANGLE class

RECTANGLE "External Users/\nSystems" as external_users

RECTANGLE "PlugIt Proxy" as proxy {
    RECTANGLE "Proxy API Endpoint" as proxy_api
    RECTANGLE "Proxy Data Store" as proxy_db
    RECTANGLE "Mail Workflow\n(Standalone Example)" as mail_workflow
}

RECTANGLE "PlugIt Service 1" as service1 {
    RECTANGLE "Service API Client" as client1
    RECTANGLE "Service Config" as config1
    RECTANGLE "Service Data Store" as db1
}

RECTANGLE "PlugIt Service 2" as service2 {
    RECTANGLE "Service API Client" as client2
    RECTANGLE "Service Config" as config2
    RECTANGLE "Service Data Store" as db2
}

RECTANGLE "PlugIt Service N" as servicen {
    RECTANGLE "Service API Client" as clientn
    RECTANGLE "Service Config" as confign
    RECTANGLE "Service Data Store" as dbn
}

RECTANGLE "Configuration Files\n(Project & Local)" as config_files
RECTANGLE "Database Technology\n(MySQL, SQLite, etc.)" as database_tech
RECTANGLE "Inbound Email\nMessages" as email_source

' Connections
external_users --> proxy_api : Access Services
proxy_api --> service1 : PlugIt Protocol
proxy_api --> service2 : PlugIt Protocol
proxy_api --> servicen : PlugIt Protocol

service1 --> client1
service1 --> config1
service1 --> db1

service2 --> client2
service2 --> config2
service2 --> db2

servicen --> clientn
servicen --> confign
servicen --> dbn

config1 --> config_files
config2 --> config_files
confign --> config_files

db1 --> database_tech
db2 --> database_tech
dbn --> database_tech

mail_workflow --> email_source : Process Inbound Mail
mail_workflow ..> proxy_api : Part of Proxy

note right of proxy
    Central access point for all services.
    Manages unified user experience.
end note

note right of service1
    Isolated service with its own
    data and configuration.
end note

note right of mail_workflow
    Standalone example workflow
    for processing inbound emails.
end note

note top of config_files
    Contains project and local
    (private) configuration settings.
end note

note bottom of database_tech
    External database provisioned
    and configured per service needs.
end note

@enduml

@startuml
title Intelligent Text Annotation System (ITAS) - Architecture

skinparam componentStyle rectangle
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
skinparam shadowing false

' Actors
actor "System Annotation\nAdministrator" as Admin
actor "Data\nAnnotator" as Annotator
actor Researcher

' Cloud and runtime
node "Tencent Cloud" as TC {
  node "Containerized Runtime (Docker)" as Docker {
    [Vue Front End\n(Vue 3 + Ant Design + Axios)] as Frontend
    [Nginx Reverse Proxy] as Nginx
    [API Gateway\n(Spring Cloud Gateway + Spring Security)] as APIGW
    [Service Registry\n(Service Discovery)] as Registry
    [Config Server] as ConfigSrv

    package "ITAS Back End\n(Spring Boot / Spring Cloud)" as Backend {
      package "Annotation Subsystem" as AnnotSub {
        [Auth Service\n(Registration/Login/Profile)] as AuthSvc
